title: Post-call: prompt pack request + recommended model targets for evaluation
preview:
From: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ravi Shah <ravi.shah@foresightlabs.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 15:07:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Quick follow-up + sample prompts from today's call

Thanks again for the demo today — appreciate the concise walkthrough. Attaching a small set of customer prompts we captured during research (foresight_sample_prompts.csv). High level follow-up items for us:

- We're primarily evaluating model fit for: customer support summarization, policy classification (safety flags), and a short answer bot for internal docs.
- Our rough SLA target: P95 latency under 400ms for single-turn short answers; for streaming summaries we care about tail latency and throughput for batch processing.
- Data sensitivity: some prompts include PII-like strings (emails, order IDs). We need guidance on redaction and how Redwood suggests collecting examples safely.

Could you share recommended model targets for an evaluation (balanced cost vs quality), a link to any relevant benchmark pages, and a short list of the exact prompt/edge-case examples you want from us?

Also looping procurement on this thread (procurement@foresightlabs.com) — they asked for a checklist for security/compliance artifacts we should expect during POC.

Attachments: foresight_sample_prompts.csv (CSV, 42 rows)

Best,
Danielle
Director of Product
Foresight Labs


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Quick follow-up + sample prompts from today's call

Danielle — thanks for sending these over and for the clear priorities. Quick recap + next steps from our side: 

Summary of what we recommend for your POC evaluation:

1) Model targets (recommended starting set):
   - redwood-13b-mixq (balanced quality/cost) — good baseline for summarization and short answers
   - redwood-3b-q4 (cost-optimized) — run on representative high-volume routes for throughput checks
   - meta-70b-std (high-quality reference) — use small sample slices for fidelity checks only (not production throughput)

2) Benchmarks and links:
   - Redwood benchmark dashboard (throughput/latency): https://console.redwood.ai/benchmarks/throughput/bench-2027-Q1
   - Example eval harness we recommend (prompt-based): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ForesightPromptPack

3) Prompt / edge-case collection guidance we need from you:
   - ~200 representative prompts per use case (support summary, policy classification, short answer). Split by typical/edge-case/intent-ambiguous examples.
   - Redaction: replace PII tokens with <REDACTED:EMAIL> / <REDACTED:ORDER_ID> and include an additional column with a boolean 'contains_pii' flag.
   - Annotate desired output for a ~25% sample so we can run automatic regression checks.

4) Quality/regression concerns to watch for during the POC:
   - Instruction-following drift after few-shot prompts — we'll run a 'control prompt' suite weekly to detect regressions.
   - Hallucination risk on internal doc short answers — include golden responses for a subset of 100 question-answer pairs.

5) Logistics & timeline:
   - If you can deliver the prompt pack and redaction flags by Tue 04/27, we can load into our eval harness and provide a preliminary model comparison by Fri 04/30.
   - Procurement checklist (initial): SOC2 report, data retention policy, schema for logs. I'll attach a short prep checklist.

Attachment: ava_prep_checklist.pdf (one-page POC intake checklist)

I'm cc'ing Aditya and Monica — Aditya will add the redaction guideline template; Monica will coordinate the eval harness run once we receive the pack.

Would you prefer the prompt pack uploaded to the Drive folder you shared, or would you like us to create a secure upload link?

Best,
Ava Chen
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: prompt pack request + recommended model targets for evaluation

Quick redaction and labeling guidance from my side — attaching a minimal template (redaction_guidelines_v1.md). A few practical notes: 

- For PII that is structural (order IDs, invoice numbers), replace with <REDACTED:ID_TYPE> and keep a separate column 'redaction_token' with the original token hashed (SHA256) if you need traceability for debugging.
- For free-text user messages that contain names/emails, replace only the exact token, do not change sentence structure; include a separate 'masking_reason' column when redaction affects context (e.g., "customer mentions doctor name").
- Edge-case examples we particularly need: ambiguous queries that require contextual follow-up, multi-turn snippets where the answer depends on prior messages, and examples with nested quoted text.

Attachments: redaction_guidelines_v1.md (Markdown, 3 sections)

I'll be available tomorrow morning PT to run through the redaction template with your data team if helpful.

—Aditya
Aditya Rao
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference


From: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ravi Shah <ravi.shah@foresightlabs.com>, procurement@foresightlabs.com, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 18:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: prompt pack request + recommended model targets for evaluation

Thanks — that is super helpful. We'll: 

- Put together the prompt pack in the Drive folder you linked and flag rows with PII using the 'contains_pii' column. Expect a first-draft upload by Mon 04/26 EOD.
- Include 150 control prompts + 100 annotated golden responses for regression checks.
- Procurement will drop SOC2 and basic SSO/KMS questions into the Drive folder as well.

One ask from procurement: can Redwood provide an explicit list of the artifacts you will need for an initial security review (exact file names/formats)? That will speed things up on our side.

Proposed mutual action plan (high level):
- 04/26: Prompt pack + initial artifacts uploaded to Drive
- 04/27: Redwood runs eval harness and returns model comparison + notes on redaction issues
- 04/30: Call to review results and pick a POC model to load into a dedicated capacity window

I'll follow up with the procurement contact on the exact artifact list. Looking forward to the eval.

Best,
Danielle
Director of Product
Foresight Labs

> 
> Attachment: ava_prep_checklist.pdf

title: IvyWorks availability exception & gating — product / ops asks
preview:
From: Emma Park <emma@ivyworks.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@ivyworks.com
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We finished a brief internal technical review and have one request before we sign: IvyWorks needs a narrowly scoped availability commitment for the /chat/generate endpoint during US business hours (M-F, 8am-6pm PT). Specifically we want a contractual p95 response-time commitment at or below 80ms for our production key path for the first 12 months.

We understand this is outside standard terms — we're open to a higher-tier Reserved / Dedicated plan and a short ramp schedule. Can you outline what Redwood would need to agree to operationally and in contract terms? I've attached our SLO spreadsheet and a one-page mutual action plan.

Best,
Emma
CTO, IvyWorks

[Attachment: IvyWorks-SLO-request.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]
[Attachment: IvyWorks-MAP-v1.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Short answer: doable with constraints. Technical notes below — high level first:

- Feasible path: Dedicated GPU pool pinned to their tenant for business-hours traffic + pre-warmed KV cache + request routing to low-latency nodes in us-west region. Expect realistic steady-state p95 ~60-90ms for small-gen prompts if we (1) reserve capacity for peak concurrency they expect and (2) prevent cold-starts during hours.
- Hard guardrails: we need a clearly defined traffic envelope (QPS, concurrency, prompt length distribution). If they spike outside agreed envelope, commitment pauses until manual approval.
- Operational asks:
  * Pre-warm schedule + synthetic load test during canary
  * Dedicated monitoring dashboard + alerting thresholds for p95 and queue length
  * Incident response SLA for paging on real p95 breach (who to oncall)
  * A fallback model variant with broader latency but lower cost (automated fallback)

Attachments: rough ops runbook sketch attached. If legal requires a monetary SLA credit we need to size it against committed reserved capacity rather than per-token.

I'll need CS numbers from Grace to give capacity sizing. Also: we should avoid blanket "sub-80ms for all endpoints" language — make it specific to /chat/generate with defined max tokens and input size.

- Marcus
Engineering — Serving Runtime

[Attachment: proposed-ops-runbook.md (text/markdown)]

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks — adding customer context. IvyWorks is a strategic SMB from our Q1 book; current daily usage is ~30k tokens/day with a predictable diurnal pattern. Expected ramp: 2-3x over 6 weeks post-launch. Peak concurrency during business hours hits ~18 concurrent sessions today. Average prompt length: 40 tokens. They are adamant about p95 for the specific path they showed in the demo (short prompt, single-turn).

Notes for negotiation: they prefer a single-line SLO in the contract rather than a long appendix. That makes our engineering ask of a narrowly-scoped SLO even more important. Suggest we offer:
  - Commitment: p95 <= 85ms for /chat/generate (max input tokens 64, max output tokens 128) during M-F 8am-6pm PT
  - Preconditions: Dedicated capacity reservation (3x current concurrency as initial commit) + 7-day warm-up with synthetic traffic + agreed-upon ramp cap and an automatic throttle if envelope exceeded
  - Measurement: Redwood's telemetry on the dedicated pool; daily digests to IvyWorks and a monthly compliance report

If legal is okay with language around "service credit proportional to reserved capacity for verifiable breaches", I think we can close quickly.

— Grace
Customer Success


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks all — flagged legal considerations below. High-level: we can accept a narrowly-scoped availability/latency commitment if the following appear in the contract:

1) Explicit scope definition (endpoint, token limits, business hours).
2) Traffic envelope and failure modes: a clause that excludes outages caused by customer-initiated traffic above the envelope, abuse, or third-party network failures.
3) Limited SLA credit: define credits as percentage of monthly reserved fee (caps + tiers) rather than per-token.
4) Exit & review cadence: 90-day operational review clause to evaluate the SLO post-ramp; right to adjust terms if we see abuse or unanticipated costs.
5) Auditability: Redwood's telemetry will be the source of truth; provide sample report format to customer.

If product and engineering approve the operational guardrails Marcus outlined, legal can draft a short SLO addendum and redline the MAP. I recommend we avoid open-ended language like "guaranteed sub-80ms" without the other constraints.

I'll prepare a first-pass SLO addendum draft once Marcus confirms capacity sizing and Grace confirms QPS ramp.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: Request: deployment surface & risk walkthrough for RFP
preview:
From: Lena Morales <lena.morales@hearthbank.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.example.com>
Cc: Hearthbank Security <security@hearthbank.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: deployment surface & risk walkthrough for RFP

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking our early RFP — I'm coordinating the security questionnaire on behalf of Hearthbank. Before we complete the formal RFI, we need a concise "deployment surface & risk" walkthrough to include in our submission:

- A one-page network diagram showing customer-facing ingress, private routing, and inter-zone egress controls.
- A short description of your isolation model for multi-tenant hosted vs. private deployments (how network, storage, and key material are segmented).
- High-level threat scenarios you consider (data exfiltration via inference endpoints, lateral movement from shared infra, compromised model weights) and your primary mitigations.
- Any evidentiary artifacts you can share (SOC2 scope statement, test-results for network/egress controls) or links to where we can validate claims.

We need this by COB Thursday (Americas hours) to make our RFP deadline. Can you send an initial package and let me know if we should schedule a 30-minute walk-through?

Appreciate it,
Lena
Lena Morales
Senior InfoSec Manager, Hearthbank


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.example.com>
To: Lena Morales <lena.morales@hearthbank.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.example.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.example.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: deployment surface & risk walkthrough for RFP

Hi Lena — thanks for the clear list. We're happy to provide an executive-ready package and can meet for a 30-minute walkthrough if helpful.

Attaching a concise packet: redwood_network_diagram_v2.pdf (single-page diagram), rfi-surface-risk-answers.xlsx (table-format responses to the four scenarios), and deployment-isolation-matrix.csv (mapping of controls by deployment mode). Summary answers below.

Quick summary (TL;DR):
- Architecture: Host the Redwood control plane in our managed region with inference workers sitting in isolated GPU pools per deployment type. Hosted customers share the control plane but run in logically isolated tenant namespaces; Dedicated and Private customers get separate VPCs/pools.
- Network ingress/egress: Customer requests enter via a regional ingress tier (TLS terminated at edge WAF), then traffic is routed over mTLS to inference gateways that live in private subnets. Outbound egress to external APIs is blocked by default; allowlists, per-tenant egress policies, and an egress proxy with TLS interception for scanning are available on request.
- Threat mitigations: KV/prefix caching obfuscation, strict RBAC and service-to-service mTLS, KMS-backed keys with HSM option for Private deployments, and continuous auditing/tracing.

We'll need one internal clarification about your SOC scope — does Hearthbank need copy of our SOC2 Type II report or just the scope statement and control list?

If that sounds right, I can schedule a walkthrough tomorrow morning PST or Thursday morning.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_network_diagram_v2.pdf (diagram), rfi-surface-risk-answers.xlsx (spreadsheet), deployment-isolation-matrix.csv (CSV)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.example.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.example.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 15:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: deployment surface & risk walkthrough for RFP

Ava / Neha — forwarding Lena's ask (below) plus the draft packet Ava attached. A couple of technical clarifications we should include in the client packet before the walkthrough:

1) Network diagram: include both logical and physical overlays — show control plane public endpoints, regional inference pools (zone-level), and optional VPC peering for Private customers. Note the default 'no outbound' posture and the egress proxy flow.

2) Isolation: for Hosted we use tenant namespaces + network policies; for Dedicated we offer per-customer virtual GPU pools with strict IAM isolation and separate persistent storage buckets with per-bucket KMS keys. For Private we should call out customer-managed network attachments and optional HSM integration.

3) Threat scenarios: add brief mitigations mapping: exfiltration -> response (egress allowlist, outbound proxy, DLP hooks); lateral movement -> segmentation + ephemeral instance policies + guardrails; model theft -> encrypted weights at rest, signing of weight artifacts, and attestation for private runtime.

I'll add two example bullet points for attestation / validation: (a) transient test harness we run during rollout (simulated API exfil), (b) audit log export via customer-managed S3 for Dedicated/Private.

If we can incorporate those, the packet should be good for Lena.

-Karthik


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.example.com>
To: Lena Morales <lena.morales@hearthbank.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.example.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.example.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: deployment surface & risk walkthrough for RFP

Hi Lena — attaching the updated package per our internal review. Notes and links follow; happy to jump on a 30-minute call tomorrow (I can do 10:00–11:30 PT).

What we attached and where to look:
- redwood_network_diagram_v2.pdf — single-page combined logical + deployment-zone diagram (see legend for control plane, regional inference pools, and optional private peering).
- rfi-surface-risk-answers.xlsx — direct answers to the scenarios you asked about with references to controls and evidence artifacts.
- deployment-isolation-matrix.csv — per-deployment-mode table: network, storage, KMS, logging, and attestation options.

Selected clarifications (call these 'executive summaries' to paste into your RFP):
1) Isolation model: Hosted — tenants are separated by namespace-level controls, network policies, and per-tenant KMS keys for persistent artifacts. Dedicated — dedicated GPU pools, per-customer S3 buckets, per-bucket KMS keys, and optional private subnets. Private (VPC/on-prem) — full network isolation with customer-managed peering and HSM/KMS integration; we support an air-gapped package for high-regulation customers.

2) Ingress & egress controls: TLS at edge, WAF, and per-route allowlists. Outbound is deny-by-default; we provide an enterprise egress proxy that enforces allowlists and optional DLP scanning. Audit logs for egress decisions are exported to customer-owned storage for Dedicated/Private configurations.

3) Threat scenarios & mitigations (short):
- Data exfiltration via API: mitigation = deny-by-default egress, egress proxy + DLP, per-tenant rate limits, audit trails; validation = simulated exfil tests (reportable).
- Lateral movement from shared infra: mitigation = strict microsegmentation, ephemeral inference workers, mTLS, and RBAC; validation = periodic internal red-team exercises and logs accessible via exports.
- Model theft / tampering: mitigation = encrypted-at-rest weights, signed model bundles, attestation during load-time for Private deployments; validation = signed-manifest checks and optional runtime attestation with customer HSMs.

Evidence availability:
- We can provide the SOC2 scope statement and a sanitized control mapping table. Full SOC2 reports are available under NDA / customer contracts; we typically provide a redacted report to prospective customers in the later stages.
- Network diagram and control mappings are in the attachments above.

Links & next steps:
- Slide walkthrough: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcdExampleLink/network-diagrams
- If you want the SOC2 under NDA, reply to this thread and we will start the sharing workflow.
- Proposed walkthrough times (PT): Fri 10:00 or Mon 09:00 — I can calendar invite once you confirm.

Thanks again, and let me know if you need anything in a different format for the RFP submission.

Regards,
Ava Chen
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 415-555-0123
Attachments: redwood_network_diagram_v2.pdf, rfi-surface-risk-answers.xlsx, deployment-isolation-matrix.csv

title: GuardianHealth RFI: SIG questionnaire clarifications & deliverable cadence
GCP co-sell Partner manager roundtable — Acme Health opportunity triage
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Singh <psingh@gcp-partner.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 09:12 AM -0700
Subject: GCP co-sell Partner manager roundtable — Acme Health opportunity triage

title: QBR prep: Alpha Solutions — exec bridge & renewal risk list

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: QBR prep: Alpha Solutions — exec bridge & renewal risk list

Team —

I pulled together an initial pack for Alpha's QBR (attached: alpha_health_snapshot.xlsx and Alpha_QBR_ask_list.md). High level: usage is drifting, NPS is soft, and procurement has started asking for SOW updates. That combination pushes this into a "renewal watch" for exec attention.

Please do the following by EOD Thu so I can synthesize for the exec bridge on Fri: 1) Laura — confirm the health indicators and add any operational incidents I missed; 2) Connor — confirm current forecasted ARR at risk and primary mitigation plan; 3) Vivek — any readiness items from infra/ops we should call out for the execs.

Key bullets to include in the briefing (draft is in Alpha_QBR_ask_list.md):
- Health snapshot: DAU/M/M, tail latency incidents, service availability (last 90d)
- Renewal risk register: 3 buckets (low/med/high) and primary owner for each row
- Exec attention items: procurement timeline, pricing ask, SLA exceptions, and a short ask for a senior-ops bridge if needed

Attachments referenced above. I will assemble a 1-page exec slide and circulation list once you send updates.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. Program Manager, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

Attachments: alpha_health_snapshot.xlsx (spreadsheet), Alpha_QBR_ask_list.md (markdown)

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR prep: Alpha Solutions — exec bridge & renewal risk list

Thanks Ava — quick pass with numbers below (pull from alpha_health_snapshot.xlsx).

Health indicators (last 30 days):
- Monthly active inference calls: down 12% vs prior month (primary drop in batch embedding workflows)
- Average 95p latency: 310ms (target 250ms) — spiking on two dates (2027-03-27, 2027-04-06) tied to model rollout events
- Uptime: 99.78% over 90 days (three short blips during scheduled infra migration windows)
- NPS from support surveys: 6.5 (weighted)

Initial risk scoring (subject to Connor confirmation):
- Renewal risk: MED-HIGH (usage decline + procurement timeline)
- Likely ask: deeper discount band or fixed-price commitment to offset unit cost

Open asks for finance: what is the committed ARR shown under ALPHA-2024-01 vs. invoiced? I don't have the exact delta for the renewal bucket.

I attached a chart for the latency trend (health_metrics_chart.png). I also highlighted two operational incidents that we should summarize for the QBR.

On the exec bridge: recommend we call out a mitigation plan (pin to dedicated pool for critical endpoints + one-week rollback window for recent model changes).

—Laura
Laura Bennett
Sr. Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR prep: Alpha Solutions — exec bridge & renewal risk list

Adding my notes and proposed mitigations. I will own outreach to the customer and align with procurement.

Forecasted ARR at risk: ~$420k (out of $1.1M ARR) based on usage trend and expected discount ask. I'll confirm after finance sync; this is a preliminary number.

Proposed mitigations (short list for exec slide):
- Quick wins (0-2 weeks): Pin production-critical routes to a dedicated pool (reduce tail latency variance), enable prefix caching for the largest batch embedding job
- Medium (2-6 weeks): Rollback the last model variant for the highest-latency pipeline and run A/B telemetry for 72 hours
- Commercial: Offer a 6-month committed tier with a modest blended discount to protect revenue while we stabilize usage

I scheduled a touchpoint with Tom for Tue Apr 27 10:00 AM PT — calendar link in related_links. I will ask procurement for an updated SOW template and draft pricing; procurement will likely request DocuSign for sign-off.

Connor O'Brien
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 16:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR prep: Alpha Solutions — exec bridge & renewal risk list

Ava / team — thanks for the heads up and the draft pack. A few quick notes from our side:

1) We're willing to do a call on Apr 27 to discuss operational fixes, but for renewal/pricing we need procurement to see a proposed SOW and revised pricing before May 5. Procurement has blocked the first week of May for discussions.
2) Can you confirm the current SLA for 95p latency and the exact language around credits? Procurement will want to see that in the SOW.
3) We noticed two regression windows after the model rollout; engineering would like the rollback plan and the telemetry you propose in writing prior to the exec call.

I'll attach SOW_questions.pdf with procurement's list. Connor — please loop procurement (procurement@alphasolutions.com) on the SOW draft when it's ready.

Best,
Tom Green
VP Engineering, Alpha Solutions

title: InvestorCare — post-call cost recap & host comparison
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-17T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Hi Samantha,

Thanks for taking time today — good call. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can prepare a focused proposal: 

High-level usage you shared
- Active users: ~12k monthly end-users (chat + structured prompts)
- Estimated monthly tokens (on current flows): 80M–140M (peak monthly)
- Expected growth: +25% q/q first two quarters after rollout

Budget / targets you mentioned
- Target monthly spend range: $1k–$4k to start (POC) but procurement has a stretch target for production budgeting
- Internal finance target: unit-rate ideally below $1,000 per 1M tokens when scaled (you said procurement would push back unless we can show economics)

Our quick shading on what that implies
- At 100M tokens/month, $1k per 1M => $100k/mo; that’s likely above your initial POC budget but aligns with production unit economics you referenced
- If we look at smaller starter pilots (10–20M/month) we can structure a credit-backed pilot to prove latency and quality before committing to a production price

Host / model cost comparison (public comps — ballpark)
- OpenAI (gpt-family hosting): ~$1.20–$4.00 per 1M tokens depending on model & mix (chat + embeddings omitted here for clarity)
- Anthropic / Claude-style hosted: ~$2.50–$6.50 per 1M tokens for higher-context models
- Open-model hosts / self-hosted (optimized runtime): $0.40–$1.50 per 1M tokens depending on quantization, batching, and caching

What we recommend for next steps
1) Can you confirm procurement’s acceptable monthly spend range for POC vs production (email to procurement cc'd is fine)?
2) Please share any non-negotiable SLA/uptime or data retention requirements — affects dedicated vs hosted pricing.
3) We’ll attach a simple cost-sensitivity sheet showing breakpoints for $/1M at 10–200M tokens and two rollout profiles (pilot -> prod).

Attached: discovery notes + cost sensitivity workbook.

I’ll loop Ben and Karthik in on a tailored pilot proposal once we get procurement’s guardrails.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: 20270417-investorcare-discovery-notes.pdf (PDF), investorcare-cost-sensitivity.xlsx (XLSX)


From: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@investorcare.com>, procurement@investorcare.com
Date: 2027-04-17T16:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Ava —

Thanks for the thorough recap. Quick clarifications from our side: 
- Procurement told me POC budget is realistically $2k–$6k for the next 60 days. Production budgeting is separate and will require a strong case (SLOs + cost projections).
- Finance meant ~$800–$1,200 per 1M tokens as a target they’d like to see for long-term pricing, not necessarily a firm commitment.
- We do have a compliance requirement for SOC2 type II evidence and an option to run in a VPC for PHI-like flows (we can share more details).

I’ll have procurement send a short note this week with the formal POC cap. Appreciate the cost sheet, will review and flag questions.

—Samantha
Head of Product, InvestorCare

On Thu, Apr 17, 2027 at 2:05 PM Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (see above)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T09:10:00-07:00
Subject: RE: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Looping in a quick internal note before we draft the pilot pricing: 

Ava — thanks for the write-up. A few thoughts on comps / positioning: 
- We should put three tiers in the pilot proposal: Hosted (fastest onboarding), Dedicated reserved (predictable latency, higher commit), Private/VPC (compliance + PHI). Pricing will differ materially.
- For the host comparisons in the outgoing sheet, let's show a 3-column view: OpenAI (public list prices), Anthropic (public), Redwood-optimized self-hosted (our projection). Use conservative runtime assumptions (60% effective batching, 40% cache hit for common prefixes) to avoid overpromising.
- Target for InvestorCare: given finance's ~800–1200 target, propose a pilot effective rate of $1,100 per 1M with credits applied (net to customer $600–$800 during POC) and a path to $500–$700 at scale (100M+/mo) — if they commit to a 6–12 month reserved capacity.

Request to Ava / Karthik:
- Ava: refine cost-sensitivity sheet to include net pricing after credits and show the break-even commit level.
- Karthik: can you draft a short two-slide technical appendix explaining batching/quantization assumptions (so procurement understands why open-host rates diverge)?

I’ll assemble the proposed pilot SOW and forward to Samantha once we have the updated sheet.

—Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference


From: procurement@investorcare.com
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>, Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@investorcare.com>
Date: 2027-04-21T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Hi all,

Confirmed POC cap: $5,000 for a 60-day pilot.
Must-haves for the POC:
- SOC2 Type II evidence or a path to provide it for the pilot (summary report is fine)
- Data isolation via VPC or clear contractual language on data residency
- A simple success metric: <30% drop in user-reported latency vs current baseline AND end-to-end false-positive rate for the main NLP classifier within 2% of current thresholds.

On pricing: procurement is comfortable with a net effective POC rate up to $1,000 per 1M tokens if credits cover the gap to our standard terms; for production we'll need a clear runway to $500–$800 per 1M at 100M+ tokens with reserved commitment.

Next step: please send a one-page pilot SOW + cost sheet with the credits applied by EOD Friday (we'd like that before our internal review next week).

Thanks,
Procurement Team
InvestorCare

title: Re: Unexpected surge on recent invoice — investigating potential duplicate consumption
preview:
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@greenbranch.health>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Nguyen <sara.nguyen@greenbranch.health>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Unexpected surge on invoice 2027-03

Hi Ava —

Hope you had a nice weekend. We just reviewed our March billing and saw a very large, unexpected jump in token consumption dated 2027-03-14 02:00–06:00 UTC. The invoice line items show what appears to be the usual baseline for our chat usage, then a sudden block of ~1.8M additional tokens charged. That surge doesn't match activity on our side — we throttled sessions during that window.

Can you:
- Confirm whether these tokens are genuine (i.e., came from our calls) or if there was a counting/replay issue on Redwood's side.
- If there was an error, outline how you will correct it and timing for a credit.

I've attached a small slice of our logs and the invoice snippet for context (greenbranch_usage_slice.csv). If you need anything else, please let me know — this is material for our finance team.

Thanks,
Tom Ellis
Head of Engineering
GreenBranch Health

Tom — thanks for the heads up and for the sample slice. I’m escalating this to our billing and engineering teams to get a root-cause and a corrective plan. Next steps from our side (I'll coordinate and keep you updated):
- Engineering will validate whether requests in that window were deduplicated or replayed.
- Billing will prepare a provisional credit if we confirm duplicated counting.
- We aim to provide a preliminary update within 48 hours and a formal credit within 5 business days if we confirm the issue.

I’ll be back with an update shortly.

—Ava

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>

Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Unexpected surge on invoice 2027-03 — GreenBranch (urgent)

Team — forwarding Tom's note and sample slice. This is a customer-critical billing question; GreenBranch is asking for confirmation and a credit if we misbilled. Please triage and propose corrective action.

What I know:
- Affected window: 2027-03-14 02:00–06:00 UTC (per customer sample)
- Customer logs indicate no heavy traffic from their app during that time
- They need a credit note and a brief RCA for their finance team

Dev — can you confirm whether any backend incident or retry/replay pattern could have doubled consumption counting for requests in that window? If you confirm duplicated counting, provide an estimate of impacted tokens and the canonical slice we should use for a credit calculation.

—Ava

---Quoted message---
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@greenbranch.health>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Unexpected surge on invoice 2027-03

(see above)


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Unexpected surge on invoice 2027-03 — GreenBranch (root cause & recommendation)

Ava/Marissa/Hannah — initial findings from the infra side: we saw a transient request-id collision in our fronting queueing layer during a rolling deploy on 2027-03-14 02:08–02:35 UTC. Two behaviors combined: (1) a rare hash collision in our short-lived request dedupe table, and (2) a downstream retry loop where a subset of RPC retries were not marked as successful before dedupe window expired. That allowed roughly 1.7M tokens to be recorded twice for a small cohort of sessions.

What we did:
- Confirmed affected request IDs by correlating timestamps and the customer's sample slice; attached a trimmed debug export (redwood_root_cause_draft.txt).
- Fixed the dedupe table expiry bug (hotfix rolled 2027-03-15 04:20 UTC) so future requests are not at risk.

Estimate and proposal:
- Estimated duplicated billing: ~1.7M tokens (±8%), which maps to $4,200 at GreenBranch's committed rate.
- Recommendation: apply a one-time credit of $4,200 to their account for the March invoice, plus a brief postmortem that explains the root cause and the fix.
- For records, we propose to base the credit on the intersection slice Tom provided (greenbranch_usage_slice.csv) which matches our debug traces.

I recommend Marissa issue the credit note and Ava send the customer a short customer-facing note with the credit and the link to our postmortem.

Attachments: redwood_root_cause_draft.txt (text, 28KB)

—Dev
Principal Software Engineer, Serving Runtime


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@greenbranch.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Resolution: credit applied for March billing spike (invoice 2027-03)

Tom — thank you for your patience while we investigated. Summary and actions we took: 

What happened
- Root cause: a rare request-id collision combined with a retry-handling gap during a rolling deploy caused a cohort of requests to be recorded twice during 2027-03-14 02:08–02:35 UTC. Engineering has deployed a hotfix to the dedupe window and the retry markers to prevent recurrence.

Customer impact and remediation
- We estimate ~1.7M duplicated tokens (±8%) for GreenBranch during the affected window.
- We have applied a one-time credit of $4,200 against invoice 2027-03. The credit has been posted to your account and will appear on your portal and the next invoice. I’ve attached a formal credit note for your finance team (credit_note_GB-2024-1023.pdf).

Next steps and transparency
- Full postmortem (internal + customer-facing summary) is available here: https://drive.redwood.com/drive/GreenBranch/postmortem-2027-04-20.
- If you or your finance team would like a different PO reference on the credit note, tell me the PO and we’ll reissue.
- We’ll follow up in two weeks with a short summary of the additional safeguards we added to the serving runtime (we've already scheduled an independent audit of the dedupe logic).

Apologies for the disruption — thank you for flagging this quickly. Please let me know if you need anything else from the team.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0194

Attachments: credit_note_GB-2024-1023.pdf (PDF, 6KB)

That allowed roughly 1.7M tokens to be recorded twice for a small cohort of sessions.

What we did:
- Confirmed affected request IDs by correlating timestamps and the customer's sample slice; attached a trimmed debug export (redwood_root_cause_draft.txt).
- Fixed the dedupe table expiry bug (hotfix rolled 2027-03-15 04:20 UTC) so future requests are not at risk.

Estimate and proposal:
- Estimated duplicated billing: ~1.7M tokens (±8%), which maps to $4,200 at GreenBranch's committed rate.
- Recommendation: apply a one-time credit of $4,200 to their account for the March invoice, plus a brief postmortem that explains the root cause and the fix.
- For records, we propose to base the credit on the intersection slice Tom provided (greenbranch_usage_slice.csv) which matches our debug traces.

I recommend Marissa issue the credit note and Ava send the customer a short customer-facing note with the credit and the link to our postmortem.

Attachments: redwood_root_cause_draft.txt (text, 28KB)

—Dev
Principal Software Engineer, Serving Runtime

- Root cause: a rare request-id collision combined with a retry-handling gap during a rolling deploy caused a cohort of requests to be recorded twice during 2027-03-14 02:08–02:35 UTC. Engineering has deployed a hotfix to the dedupe window and the retry markers to prevent recurrence.

Customer impact and remediation

title: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sana Roy <sana.roy@medicore.com>, Timothy Park <tpark@medicore.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Hi Sana / Tim —

Thanks again for a helpful call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we have alignment before we spin up the trial environment. I pulled these points from our conversation; please flag anything I missed.

Summary of goals (what success looks like for Medicore):
- Verify end-to-end latency for query-heavy workflows under 60ms p50 and acceptable p95 for peak windows.
- Validate model quality on your diagnostic prompt set: >= 92% pass rate on intent extraction and <5% hallucination on the clinical QA set.
- Confirm telemetry and audit log fidelity (token-level tracing + request IDs) for compliance review.
- Exercise failover path (hosted -> dedicated sandbox) under simulated capacity limits.

Trial scope & environment options discussed:
1) Quick path: Hosted API sandbox (Redwood-hosted) — fastest to get started, minimal lift; best for early quality and latency baselines.
2) Dedicated sandbox (reserved GPUs in our staging cluster) — predictable throughput for load testing; recommended if you want to validate throughput at scale.
3) VPC trial (Private) — for evaluation with your network controls and KMS; requires network onboarding and ~2–3 weeks to complete.

Recommended initial plan (we suggest a 3-week exploratory trial):
- Week 0 (this week): Kickoff, access + sample prompt exchange, share evaluation prompt set and success criteria.
- Week 1: Hosted baseline runs (quality + latency) — Redwood provides host endpoints and initial eval dashboard.
- Week 2: If throughput testing is needed, switch to Dedicated sandbox for peak/scale tests.
- Week 3: Optional VPC onboarding for security review (parallel preparatory work can start week 1).

Owners & immediate asks:
- Medicore: share your evaluation prompt sets (clinical QA + extraction), and a list of sensitive fields for masking (Sana / Tim).
- Redwood AE (Ava): coordinate kickoff, schedule demo for engineering team, manage billing/trial limits.
- Redwood Solutions (Ben): SOC2 artifacts, trial SOW and monitoring dashboard configuration.
- Redwood Ops (Marcus): provisioning Dedicated sandbox if requested; network checklist for VPC.

Attachments: I attached a first-pass Method of Procedure (Medicore_MoP_v1.pdf) and a trial SOW draft to accelerate procurement.

Proposed immediate next step: 30-minute kickoff on Tue 4/19 to exchange prompt sets and finalize which environment to start with — are you available 10:00–10:30am PT?

Thanks,

Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
Redwood Inference | Developer-first inference platform

title: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Team —

I pulled together a first-pass migration lane matrix for the v1 -> rfv2 model transition and an initial proposal for an "escape-hatch" policy for customers who need more time. Attached: migration_lane_matrix.csv and a short comm draft (comm_draft.md).

Key asks: 
- Product: confirm which model variants we must keep in the slow-lane (compatibility) for 90 days vs 30 days.
- Sales/CS: flag customers who need exemption (we currently have a few in legal procurement) and prioritize by revenue/impact.
- Infra/Runtime (Marcus): are there known perf regressions on rfv2 at <64 token sequences we should call out in comms?

My proposed lanes in the matrix: 
- Fast lane: auto-migrate at day 0, full deprecation fallback after 30 days
- Slow lane: 90-day soft support, manual opt-in migration, technical support priority
- Escape-hatch: ad-hoc 120-day exceptions for validated compliance/regulatory cases (manual review)

I want us to land on a policy by end-of-day Tuesday (2027-04-20) so we can start customer outreach Wednesday. Please reply inline with your lane suggestions and any required policy text.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Attachment: migration_lane_matrix.csv (csv)
Attachment: comm_draft.md (markdown)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

On Sun, Apr 18, 2027 at 08:12 AM Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> I pulled together a first-pass migration lane matrix for the v1 -> rfv2 model transition...

Thanks for pulling this together, Ava. Quick product notes after skimming the matrix: 

1) Compatibility surface: rfv2 removes the legacy reranking endpoint semantics (scoring mode). Any customer using the old /rerank path will need a shim or explicit rerun. We should add a compatibility flag (query param) that we can support for 30 days to avoid breaking customers in the fast lane.

2) Latency/regressions: Marcus — I've seen 5-8% higher p95 for 1-16 token sequences on A100 when quantized; if infra can't fix that quickly, call it out as a risk in comms. If we offer a per-customer small fast-path reservation (dedicated 1-slot burst) we can keep latency SLOs for key accounts.

3) Escape-hatch gating criteria: propose a 3-step validation: (a) sales attestation of customer impact, (b) security/compliance check (if data residency/regulatory), (c) PM approval. Use the customer_exemptions_list.xlsx to capture approvals.

I added inline comments in the matrix and flagged two customers (OakWallet and Olympus Payments) as likely requests.

-Karthik
Head of Product, Serving
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Ava / team —

Flagging customer context from the field:
- OakWallet (Katie Ham, katie.ham@oakwallet.com): heavy use of embeddings + long-running session state. They asked explicitly for a 90-day slow lane previously; legal is pushing back on new SLAs unless we provide a concrete rollback plan.
- Olympus Payments (support@olympus-payments.com): mid-sized, high-volume. Ops is nervous about token-cost regressions; they want an explicit burst reservation for peak hours.

Suggested sales-side commitments: 
- Offer OakWallet a timed escape-hatch (90 -> 120 days) if they sign a migration support addendum (1-2 week dedicated onboarding from Solutions Eng).
- For customers with cost concerns, offer a brief cost-analysis workshop and a one-week rollback window during initial migration.

I uploaded a draft exemption spreadsheet (customer_exemptions_list.xlsx) with the two accounts noted and preliminary revenue tiers. Also added a short draft email we can send once policy approved.

Laura Bennett
Senior AE, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: customer_exemptions_list.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 17:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Appreciate the input. A few roadmap/comms asks from PM side: 

- Timeline: We need formal canary dates for the first 10% of traffic, and a rollback plan that is executed automatically (feature flag + model routing) in case of severe regressions. Proposed schedule:
  * Week 0 (Apr 21): enable canary for internal traffic and Redwood demo orgs
  * Week 1 (Apr 28): 10% customer traffic (fast lane only) + monitor quality metrics
  * Week 3 (May 12): begin slow-lane invitations for selected customers with SE support

- Messaging: comm_draft.md needs a clear "what to expect" section and explicit SLOs for any customers granted escape-hatch. We should also add an FAQ for the outages/rollback process.

- Exceptions: We should limit active escape-hatches to no more than 8 customers at a time to keep support overhead manageable. Add that constraint to the matrix.

Marcus — can you confirm whether the runtime team can make the temporary compatibility-flag available for fast-lane rollback? If not, we should escalate to infra leadership today.

-Marissa
Group PM, Model Infra
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Thanks everyone — good alignment. Short summary and decisions: 

1) Lanes finalized: fast (auto, 30d rollback window), slow (90d), escape-hatch (120d limited to 8 active customers).
2) Compatibility flag: Marcus confirms runtime will ship a temporary query param flag for /generate and /chat routes by Thu EOD (2027-04-22).
3) Sales commitments: Laura to get OakWallet to sign the addendum; if OakWallet requests 120d, they must agree to a 2-week SE onboarding and a migration checklist.
4) Comms: Ava will publish a one-page FAQ + comm_draft.md to Drive and send a sales-ready email template by Wed morning.

Action items: 
- Laura: update customer_exemptions_list.xlsx and capture signatures (due 2027-04-26).
- Marcus: confirm rollout plan and post canary runbook (due 2027-04-22).
- Karthik/Marissa: finalize the compatibility gating language in the policy doc (due 2027-04-21).

I'll send the customer note once we have the SE addendum for OakWallet. Thanks all.

– Ava
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3-migration-playbooks (comm_draft.md, canary-runbook)

Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quarterly board pack — pipeline consolidation & account dossiers

Team,

As we finalize the QBR board pack I need a concise, account-by-account consolidation of the sales pipeline for the board slides. I attached a working workbook (QBR_board_pack_pipeline_v1.xlsx) and a lightweight account dossier template (account_dossiers_template.md). Please do the following by EOD Tuesday (Apr 19):

- For each account you own, fill the dossier with: current ARR (annualized), committed close date, forecasted close bucket (Committed/Best Case/Pipeline), confidence (H/M/L), next milestone, blockers, and one-line executive summary.
- Update the pipeline tab in the workbook with a single row per opportunity. Keep notes short — 1–2 bullets per account.
- Flag any legal/compliance blockers (we may need to push to Legal or Security).

Why this matters: board pack needs a clean runway slide with consolidated numbers and two account spotlights that represent upside and risk. I will synthesize into the final deck; Tessa will present next week.

Attachments: QBR_board_pack_pipeline_v1.xlsx (workbook), account_dossiers_template.md (template).

If you're going to miss the deadline, ping me now so we can reassign.

Ava

title: Renewal: reserved GPU increase & updated per-hour terms
preview:
From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@vectorcloud.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Hi Ava,

Hope you had a great week. We're coming up on our renewal on May 1 and are evaluating moving from our current 16-GPU reserved pool to a 32-GPU pool for the next 12 months. Growth in inference volume has been faster than we forecasted, and our latency-sensitive endpoints need the additional headroom and KV-cache improvements.

A few asks up front:
- Can you share an updated reserved per-hour rate for 32 GPUs (12-month term) and the incremental discount thresholds?
- Is there a throughput SLO or baseline you can commit to once we double capacity? We currently see occasional token throttling during morning spikes.
- Any recommended change to burst capacity / autoscale settings for predictable cost?

We have procurement and security reviews starting next week. If you can send a proposed SOW + pricing, we can kick off internal approvals.

Thanks,
Jordan
Jordan Ellis
Head of Platform Ops
VectorCloud
[Attachment: vectorcloud_current_usage_q1.csv (csv, 58KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Hi Jordan — great to hear from you and congrats on the growth. We can absolutely support a move to a 32-GPU reserved pool for your renewal.

High level:
- I'll attach a proposed SOW and a pricing sheet for a 12-month reserved term here. The sheet includes two pricing bands: a standard reserved per-hour and a committed-throughput tier with tighter per-token economics.
- On throughput SLOs: for a 32-GPU reserved pool we can guarantee 99.5% availability at your current median sequence length and provide a throughput baseline of ~1.8x your current sustained tokens/sec (we'll include the measurement assumptions). For spikes, we recommend enabling a 2-GPU short-burst buffer (billed on usage) to absorb 30–60s surges.
- Autoscale: we suggest a conservative warm-up policy (add 2 GPUs at 65% CPU/GPU mem) with prefetching of hot prefixes — Marcus can add notes on KV-cache sizing.

Next steps:
1) Neha will follow up with the formal pricing sheet and SOW draft.
2) If procurement wants a formal throughput SLA in the contract, let us know the language you need; we can propose SLA metrics and credits.
3) Marcus will validate the workload profile and confirm the burst recommendation.

Attaching: VectorCloud-Renewal-SOW.pdf (draft) and Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (pricing bands).

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: VectorCloud-Renewal-SOW.pdf (pdf, 42KB)]
[Attachment: Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (xlsx, 18KB)]

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs) — proposed commercial terms

Jordan — following up with proposed commercial terms for the 12-month reserved pool upgrade. Key items in the attached pricing sheet and SOW: 

Commercial highlights:
- 32-GPU reserved pool, 12-month fixed term.
- Reserved per-hour rate: $14.60 per GPU-hour (applies to core reserved capacity).
- Committed-throughput tier: $13.40 per GPU-hour if you commit to a monthly usage floor equal to 90% of historical average (captures heavy steady-state customers).
- Burst buffer: short-burst GPUs billed at on-demand rate of $3.20 per GPU-hour (metered in 1-minute increments).
- One-time migration assistance credit: $3,500 (applied to month 1 invoice).

SLA and throughput: we propose the following clause (sample):
"Redwood will maintain 99.5% availability for the reserved pool measured on a monthly basis. Throughput baseline will be defined using the baseline profile in Appendix A; credits of 5% of monthly reserved fees will apply for monthly availability below threshold up to three consecutive months."

Notes: Marcus confirmed the KV-cache sizing needed for roughly 30% reduced p99 latency once the pool is resized and warm cache policies are applied. Marcus — can you confirm the engineering notes for Appendix A?

If this looks good, we can:
- Finalize SOW and send to your procurement for redlines.
- Lock pricing for 7 days pending signature to align with your May 1 renewal date.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call this week with procurement to walk through the SLA/terms.

Regards,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (xlsx, 18KB)]

From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@vectorcloud.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Thanks Neha — appreciate the clear breakdown. A couple quick clarifications from procurement: 

1) Can the committed-throughput tier be applied retroactively on the invoice if we exceed the usage floor mid-month, or does it require a prior-month commitment? Procurement prefers retroactive true-ups to avoid forecasting risk.
2) We want the burst buffer cap to be configurable; does Redwood allow a monthly cap on burst spend? If so, add that as an Appendix item.
3) The SLA credit (5%) — can we instead get a service credit ladder (5% for one month, 10% if repeated two months) up to a cap of 30%?

If you can confirm answers to 1–3 and send a clean SOW with those potential edits, we can route for signature. We may also ask for a short 30-minute call to align on Appendix A measurements.

Jordan
Jordan Ellis
Head of Platform Ops, VectorCloud

We're kicking off the renewal for our Redwood services and procurement needs the SOC 2 Type II report (latest period), plus any interim/bridge documentation you can provide. Our renewal effective date is 2027-05-01 and procurement prefers to have final evidence or an accepted bridge letter in place before issuing the PO.

title: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mike Rhodes <mike.rhodes@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Team —

Following the convo with Acme (Mike copied) and a couple of other enterprise prospects, we have a clear ask: customers want an at-a-glance view on Console home that surfaces both latency and spend signals per tenant (or billing tag). The idea is compact \"small-multiples\" tiles — one row per tenant, showing p50/p95 latency sparkline and a spend bar for the selected window (default: 7d). Key goals:

- Detect tenant regressions quickly (spike in p95)
- Correlate latency shifts with spend changes (e.g., sudden burst due to traffic or pricing anomaly)
- Provide quick drill paths: click a tenant -> per-route breakdown, recent errors, and last 24h traces

I attached a quick mock (console-home-tiles-mock.png). High-level product asks:
- Tiles limited to top-20 tenants by spend, with a search to pin any tenant
- 7d default window, 1h aggregation for sparkline; ability to switch to 24h/30d
- Spend attribution: show tokens billed and estimated $ (using current price mapping)
- Toggle to show p50/p95 or both

Questions for engineering/analytics:
1) What rollups do we need (granularity, retention) to support 1h/1m views?
2) Can we derive spend quickly from existing billing events or do we need a new aggregation job?
3) Any perf concerns for a Console home query that may show 20 tenants concurrently?

Requesting: a short feasibility note + rough ETA for an MVP (top-10 tenants, p95 + spend, 7d) by EOD Wednesday 4/14.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Console
Redwood Inference

Attachment: console-home-tiles-mock.png (image/png)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Mike Rhodes <mike.rhodes@acme.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Ava —

Love this — a few clarifying items from CS/field perspective:

- For \"tenant\" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
- Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
- Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.

Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?

On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.

- Marissa

> Product Manager, Console

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.

Data assumptions and needs:
- Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
  - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
- Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

Suggested MVP scope:
- Build 1h rollups for top-20 tenants (weekly job to recompute top-20) + realtime windowed aggregator to keep a 5m p95 for only top-10 tenants.
- Expose an API endpoint /api/console/tiles/tenants that returns cached JSON per-home-view (recompute cache every 1m for top tenants).

Effort rough estimate:
- Rollup pipelines (ETL + infra): 2 engineers x 2 weeks
- API + cache: 1 engineer x 1 week
- Frontend tiles + drill UX: 1 FE engineer x 1 week (MVP limited visuals)
- Backfill scripts and testing: 1 engineer x 4 days

Storage/cost impact: expect additional storage for 5m rollups ~50GB/month for global tenant set; limiting to top-20 reduces that by ~10x. Also small incremental compute for streaming aggregator.

I attached a sample query that would power the API (cost-latency-rollup-proposal.sql).

Priyom
Senior Data Engineer, Platform Metrics
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Apr 13, 2027 at 10:02 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Ava —
> 
> Love this — a few clarifying items from CS/field perspective:
> 
> - For \"tenant\" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
> - Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
> - Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.
> 
> Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?
> 
> On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.
> 
> - Marissa


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Putting on my infra hat — a couple of notes and a recommended constrained MVP: 

- Live compute: computing 5m p95 for *all* tenants at 1m cadence is expensive. We should scope an MVP to top-10 tenants and provide a soft-fail for "others" (show last computed value + link to per-tenant page for on-demand compute).
- Cache strategy: use a read-through cache with 1m TTL for top tenants; fall back to on-demand worker for pinned tenants. This will keep Console home QPS affordable.
- Backfill: if we want historical sparklines beyond the cache window we need a backfill job; estimate 3 days effort to backfill 90d for top-20 using the offline rollups.

MVP timeline (assuming Priyom's estimates):
- Week 1: ETL + small rollup infra + API scaffolding
- Week 2: FE tiles + API integration + cache tuning
- Week 3: testing, backfill and polish, docs

If everyone is aligned I can spin up the cache and allocate infra quota for a 2-week sprint.

- Dylan

- For \"tenant\" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
- Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
- Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.

Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?

On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.

- Marissa

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 09:14 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Following the convo with Acme (Mike copied) and a couple of other enterprise prospects, we have a clear ask: customers want an at-a-glance view on Console home that surfaces both latency and spend signals per tenant (or billing tag). The idea is compact \"small-multiples\" tiles — one row per tenant, showing p50/p95 latency sparkline and a spend bar for the selected window (default: 7d). Key goals:
> 
> - Detect tenant regressions quickly (spike in p95)
> - Correlate latency shifts with spend changes (e.g., sudden burst due to traffic or pricing anomaly)
> - Provide quick drill paths: click a tenant -> per-route breakdown, recent errors, and last 24h traces
> 
> I attached a quick mock (console-home-tiles-mock.png). High-level product asks:
> - Tiles limited to top-20 tenants by spend, with a search to pin any tenant
> - 7d default window, 1h aggregation for sparkline; ability to switch to 24h/30d
> - Spend attribution: show tokens billed and estimated $ (using current price mapping)
> - Toggle to show p50/p95 or both
> 
> Questions for engineering/analytics:
> 1) What rollups do we need (granularity, retention) to support 1h/1m views?
> 2) Can we derive spend quickly from existing billing events or do we need a new aggregation job?
> 3) Any perf concerns for a Console home query that may show 20 tenants concurrently?
> 
> Requesting: a short feasibility note + rough ETA for an MVP (top-10 tenants, p95 + spend, 7d) by EOD Wednesday 4/14.
> 
> Thanks,
> Ava Chen
> Product Manager, Console
> Redwood Inference


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.

Data assumptions and needs:
- Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
  - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
- Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

Suggested MVP scope:
- Build 1h rollups for top-20 tenants (weekly job to recompute top-20) + realtime windowed aggregator to keep a 5m p95 for only top-10 tenants.
- Expose an API endpoint /api/console/tiles/tenants that returns cached JSON per-home-view (recompute cache every 1m for top tenants).

Effort rough estimate:
- Rollup pipelines (ETL + infra): 2 engineers x 2 weeks
- API + cache: 1 engineer x 1 week
- Frontend tiles + drill UX: 1 FE engineer x 1 week (MVP limited visuals)
- Backfill scripts and testing: 1 engineer x 4 days

Storage/cost impact: expect additional storage for 5m rollups ~50GB/month for global tenant set; limiting to top-20 reduces that by ~10x. Also small incremental compute for streaming aggregator.

I attached a sample query that would power the API (cost-latency-rollup-proposal.sql).

Priyom
Senior Data Engineer, Platform Metrics
Redwood Inference

> - Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.
> 
> Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?
> 
> On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.
> 
> - Marissa


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Putting on my infra hat — a couple of notes and a recommended constrained MVP: 

- Live compute: computing 5m p95 for *all* tenants at 1m cadence is expensive. We should scope an MVP to top-10 tenants and provide a soft-fail for "others" (show last computed value + link to per-tenant page for on-demand compute).
- Cache strategy: use a read-through cache with 1m TTL for top tenants; fall back to on-demand worker for pinned tenants. This will keep Console home QPS affordable.
- Backfill: if we want historical sparklines beyond the cache window we need a backfill job; estimate 3 days effort to backfill 90d for top-20 using the offline rollups.

MVP timeline (assuming Priyom's estimates):
- Week 1: ETL + small rollup infra + API scaffolding
- Week 2: FE tiles + API integration + cache tuning
- Week 3: testing, backfill and polish, docs

If everyone is aligned I can spin up the cache and allocate infra quota for a 2-week sprint.

- Dylan

title: Private connectivity & on-prem bundle — implementation playbook
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@heliostx.com>, Marco Santoro <marco.santoro@heliostx.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 05 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private connectivity & on-prem bundle — implementation playbook

Hi Elena / Marco —

Thanks again for choosing Redwood for the private deployment. As discussed on the call last week, I wanted to kick off the implementation playbook so your network and security teams have a single reference. Below is the proposed scope and immediate asks so we can lock dates for the on-prem package install and VPC peering validation.

Scope (short):
- Private connectivity: AWS VPC peering (primary), with optional VPN fallback for on-prem.
- Private endpoints for control plane APIs and telemetry collectors.
- On-prem package: installer tarball + hardware checklist (for air-gapped sites).
- Key & secret management integration (KMS/HSM).

Immediate asks from Helios networking/security team:
1) Confirm the AWS account IDs we should peer with and which VPC/subnet will accept the peering (attach route table CIDR if possible).
2) Provide the public IP range for the NAT/transit gateway if you expect to use egress rules for telemetry.
3) Indicate whether you will require site-to-site VPN as primary or only as fallback.
4) Point of contact for HSM/KMS and any required certificates.

Proposed timeline (workable draft):
- This week (Apr 5–9): Exchange account IDs & network CIDRs; Redwood shares on-prem package spec.
- Week of Apr 12: Network validation & peering test in staging.
- Week of Apr 19: On-prem package installation window (2–4 hours), post-install smoke tests.

Attachments: redwood-onprem-package-spec.pdf (installer details), sample network diagram.

If the above looks good, can you confirm the two primary points of contact and whether Apr 19–23 works for the install window?

Thanks — I’ll coordinate SOW finalization once the timeline is agreed.

Best,
Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood-onprem-package-spec.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB)]

Elena / team —

Appreciate the quick responses and the sheet. Wed Apr 7 10:00 PT works for our network validation session — I’ll be on the call to walk through our peering checklist. A few prep notes for the call so we can be actionable: 

Pre-call checklist (please confirm on the call or beforehand):
- Confirm that the Helios VPC has no overlapping CIDRs with any of your on-prem zones or transit gateways (we saw 10.48.0.0/16 — confirm no overlap with your data center 10.48.0.0/16).
- Attach the route table that will be updated by peering (send the RT ID).
- Share the acceptance window for the peering (who will click "Accept" and what is the expected SLA for acceptance).
- If using transit gateway, confirm whether route propagation will be used vs static routes.

On the KMS/HSM front: Marco, two quick questions for you before the install:
- Will Helios provide an HSM endpoint reachable from the Redwood installer, or do you expect us to integrate with your KMS via cross-account role and key policy?

Installer & rollback notes:
- The on-prem package is non-destructive by default — we run in a staging container and require an internal approval step before activating traffic routing.
- We will snapshot existing config and keep a 2-hour rollback window documented in runbook.

I'll put the staging peering validation runbook in the shared Drive after the call.

—Vivek
Senior Platform Engineer, Redwood Inference
