title: Dedicated capacity options for inference — pilot & staged activation
preview:
From: Claire Park <claire.park@nimblehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Jensen <ajensen@nimblehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity options for inference (pilot & staged activation)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We're lining up the architecture and procurement plan for an inference product (clinical decision support) and need to understand Redwood's dedicated deployment options. Key things we care about:

- Recommended cluster sizing for a 95th percentile concurrency of ~2.5k reqs/min with burst to 4k/min for 30–60s windows
- Minimum live/reserved GPUs required to keep KV cache efficiency and latency SLOs (p99 target ~250ms)
- A safe ramp schedule (pilot -> growth -> steady state) that balances cost and warm-up risk
- Any warm-pool / pre-warming strategies and how they’re billed

We are procurement-led, so visibility on pricing bands (pilot credits, minimum committed lanes, and discount thresholds) would help. We are in the US region and hoping to kick a pilot in early April.

Can you send a few concrete options (cluster sizes, min GPUs, ramp timeline) plus any operational notes about cache/warm-up behavior?

Thanks,
Claire
Claire Park
Head of Platform Partnerships
Nimble Health



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Park <claire.park@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options for inference (pilot & staged activation)

Hi Claire —

Appreciate the details. Attached are three pragmatic activation pathways tailored to the traffic profile you shared. High-level summary below; detailed pricing & timeline is in nimble-dedicated-options-v1.pdf and activation-timeline.xlsx (attached).

Option A — Short pilot (conservative)
- Pilot footprint: 8 GPU-equivalent lanes (A100/80GB-equivalent) for a 30-day pilot
- Min reserved: 8 (pilot-only) -> billed as reserved for the pilot window
- Ramp plan: pilot (M0) for 30d -> optional expand to 16 GPUs in month 2 if latency/throughput validated
- Use-case: validate prompt sets, KV cache hit rate, and initial failover behavior

Option B — Staged growth (recommended for predictable production)
- Pilot: 12 GPUs for 30 days (validation + 10% headroom)
- Minimum committed: 24 GPUs after month 3 (reserved), with autoscale to burst lanes up to 48 for traffic spikes
- Ramp schedule: 12 (pilot) -> 24 (month 3) -> 48 (month 6)
- Warm-pool: 10% idle warm pool retained inside the committed pool to preserve KV cache and reduce cold-start latency

Option C — Aggressive committed posture (for performance-critical products)
- Immediate reserved baseline: 48 GPUs, min reserved 24 during the first 60 days while we tune models
- Ramp to 96 GPUs by month 9 as traffic and models scale
- Best for customers who want strict latency SLOs and capacity predictability

Operational notes
- KV cache effectiveness: We recommend at least 12–16 GPUs active to get stable cache hit ratios for long-context models; very small pilots (<=8 GPUs) can have lower steady-state cache hit rates and higher cold-token cost.
- Autoscaling: available but it’s subject to warm-up delays for very large model weights; we recommend burst lanes for traffic spikes rather than relying on instantaneous scale-to-zero.
- Billing: reserved GPUs billed monthly; warm-pool usage is billed as part of the reserved seat. Burst lanes are billed at an overage/burst rate.

We can include a 30-day pilot credit and a staged discount schedule (5% at 12 GPUs reserved for 6 months, 12% at 24+ for 12 months) — will confirm with finance once you indicate a preferred path.

If you like Option B we can propose a concrete month-by-month activation plan and draft terms.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachments: nimble-dedicated-options-v1.pdf (proposal), activation-timeline.xlsx (timeline matrix)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Park <claire.park@nimblehealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options for inference (pilot & staged activation)

Claire — quick engineering notes to complement Ava's options below.

1) Min live GPUs and cache behavior
- For large context LLMs (8k+ tokens), we see cache warm-up curves improve significantly once you have ~12–16 concurrently live lanes serving the same model family. Below that, the working set frequently misses and you pay more in token compute.

2) Autoscaling & pre-warm strategy
- We suggest a small warm pool (10–15% of reserved) to keep KV shards resident. The warm pool is charged as reserved capacity but avoids the latency spikes of cold starts.

3) Model sharding and memory footprint
- If you plan to run multiple model sizes, plan for headroom: e.g., one 8xA100 cluster can host ~3x medium-sized models before memory pressure forces extra nodes.

4) SLA and 99th-percentile latency
- We target 99th < 350ms on committed clusters with warm-pool enabled; hitting 250ms p99 requires a larger reserved baseline and higher burst capacity.

Happy to join a technical call to walk the team through the warm-up telemetry and how we'll instrument cache hit-rate dashboards.

-ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Claire Park <claire.park@nimblehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Jensen <ajensen@nimblehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options for inference (pilot & staged activation)

Thanks Ava/Ben — this is very helpful. A few follow-ups from procurement & engineering: 

- We are price sensitive early on. Can you soften the suggested minimum to 12 reserved GPUs (instead of 24) with an obligation to step to 24 by month 4 conditional on meeting agreed traffic/latency thresholds?
- What level of pilot credits do you typically provide? A full 30-day credit on the first 12 GPUs would be ideal to accelerate internal validation.
- Would Redwood accept a 90-day rollback clause if model performance materially underperforms (we need a short-term exit window while still being practical)?

Alex (cc'd) is the procurement lead and looking to understand discount triggers and PO process.

Appreciate the quick turn — can we lock a kickoff for next Tuesday?

Claire



From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Park <claire.park@nimblehealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 05 Apr 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options for inference (pilot & staged activation)

Claire/Alex — thanks for the clear ask. Proposed commercial/activation compromise below (happy to refine):

- Pilot phase (30 days): 12 GPUs reserved, Redwood provides a one-time 100% credit on reserved charges for the pilot (credit applies to first 30d only).
- Validation window: at end of pilot we evaluate 2 KPIs (p99 latency & average request rate). If both targets met, Nimble escalates to the staged commit.
- Staged commit (months 2–4): increase to 24 GPUs reserved by month 3. If KPIs are not met, Nimble may choose to extend pilot for 30d or exit with a 90-day rollback right (refund/credit mechanics defined in SOW).
- Long-term ramp: schedule to reach 48 GPUs by month 6 contingent on usage growth; commitment discounts: 8% @ 12 GPU commit (12+ months), 14% @ 24 GPU commit (12+ months), 20% @ 48 GPU commit (12+ months).
- Early-exit and credits: 90-day operational rollback available in first 6 months; finance will model pro-rata credits for unused reserved capacity under the rollback clause.

Operationally we'll reserve a small warm pool (~10%) inside the reserved seats to preserve KV cache and reduce cold latency. We will also provide dashboard access (token-level burn, cache hit rates, per-route latency).

If this looks good, we'll generate a redline SOW and a DocuSign link. Proposed deal id: DEAL-5842.

- Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
Head of Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference

Links: DocuSign (https://app.docusign.com/embeddedSigning?doc=DS-9843)


From: Claire Park <claire.park@nimblehealth.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Jensen <ajensen@nimblehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options for inference (pilot & staged activation)

Rishi/Ava — this aligns with our internal tolerance. Nimble is prepared to proceed with the 30-day pilot on 12 reserved GPUs with the pilot credit and the staged commit path you outlined. We've processed a PO stub (nimble-po-4301.pdf) for administrative purposes and Claire-sign-off.

Next steps:
1) Please send the SOW and DocuSign link (we'll circulate to legal/procurement)
2) Schedule a technical kickoff (we prefer next Wednesday, 10am PT) — include Ben for the warm-pool/cache discussion
3) Share the activation-timeline.xlsx populated with expected activation dates (we will sync the internal release schedule)

Thanks — excited to get started.

Claire Park
Head of Platform Partnerships | Nimble Health

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@finly.com>

Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — schedule options

Hi Samantha —

Thanks again for the interest in running a hands-on kickoff with the routing policy team. We'd like to get a 90-minute working session on the calendar to do the following:

Agenda (90 min)
- 15m: Quick intros & success criteria (what Finly needs from the POC)
- 25m: Prompt walk-through and promptsets we should test
- 25m: Routing policy decisions (model tiers, fallback rules, region mapping)
- 20m: Load-test plan and next steps (dry-run timing, traffic profile, observability points)

Proposed session windows (pick one):
- Tue Apr 6 — 10:00–11:30 PT / 13:00–14:30 ET
- Wed Apr 7 — 15:00–16:30 PT / 18:00–19:30 ET
- Thu Apr 8 — 07:00–08:30 PT / 10:00–11:30 ET (our preference; mid-morning ET)

Pre-work
- If you can, please share a sample traffic profile (percentages by endpoint, typical request sizes) and any redlines for latency/cost.
- We attached a short runbook (rti-runbook-v0.9.pdf) with proposed roles for the session.

Logistics
- We'll run the hands-on session with a shared Drive doc for prompts and a short load plan for the dry-run. If you want to loop in DevOps or infra, please cc them when you reply.

Would Thu Apr 8 at 10:00am ET work for you? If not, tell us which of the other two slots is best.

Thanks —
Ava
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — initial prework

Ava / team —

Putting together a short model matrix to bring to the session. Attached is a draft shortlist (model_shortlist.xlsx) with recommended default fallback order and quantization options. High level suggestions:

- Tier 1 (low-latency, higher cost): Redwood-optimized gpt-4o-small-quant — good for critical endpoints with strict latency SLAs.
- Tier 2 (balanced): vetted open LLM v1.3 (8-bit) — good quality/cost tradeoff for general traffic.
- Tier 3 (economic): Claude-lite / instruction-tuned small models — best for background and batch tasks.

Fallback policy (proposed defaults)
- If Tier 1 latency >= 200ms P50 or a 5% error spike, failover to Tier 2 for 60s with circuit-breaker backoff.
- Region mapping: prefer region nearest to customer; if capacity constrained, route to secondary region within same GDPR boundary.

Rohan — can you check capacity and whether we can reserve a small dedicated pool for the dry-run on Apr 7? Connor, Evelyn — please flag any models you think shouldn't be on the shortlist.

Marcus
Lead, Inference Ops
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Subject: Re: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — initial prework

Ava / team —

Putting together a short model matrix to bring to the session. Attached is a draft shortlist (model_shortlist.xlsx) with recommended default fallback order and quantization options. High level suggestions:

- Tier 1 (low-latency, higher cost): Redwood-optimized gpt-4o-small-quant — good for critical endpoints with strict latency SLAs.
- Tier 2 (balanced): vetted open LLM v1.3 (8-bit) — good quality/cost tradeoff for general traffic.
- Tier 3 (economic): Claude-lite / instruction-tuned small models — best for background and batch tasks.

Fallback policy (proposed defaults)
- If Tier 1 latency >= 200ms P50 or a 5% error spike, failover to Tier 2 for 60s with circuit-breaker backoff.
- Region mapping: prefer region nearest to customer; if capacity constrained, route to secondary region within same GDPR boundary.

Rohan — can you check capacity and whether we can reserve a small dedicated pool for the dry-run on Apr 7? Connor, Evelyn — please flag any models you think shouldn't be on the shortlist.

title: Hedgewell discovery — next steps & proposed onramp
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Lena Park <lena.park@hedgewell.com>, Mark Dunn <mark.dunn@hedgewell.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hedgewell discovery — next steps & proposed onramp

Hi Lena / Mark —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our discovery call and a proposed lightweight onramp so everyone is aligned on next steps. I kept this intentionally short.

Key pains you called out:
- Latency spikes on peak inference windows (search/QA flows) causing customer-facing errors.
- High per-token costs with current multi-tenant setup; need predictable unit economics for pilot.
- Compliance: certain datasets need EU residency; you also mentioned stricter audit/log retention for financial workloads.

Primary goals for the pilot:
1) Reduce per-token inference cost by ~3x relative to current spend for the same quality profile.
2) Maintain P95 latency under 300ms for typical requests and stable behavior during bursts.
3) End-to-end integration with your SSO + KMS; logs retained for 90 days and exportable for audits.

Stakeholders we captured:
- Product: Lena Park (Primary)
- Engineering lead: Mark Dunn (Integration + SRE)
- Procurement/Legal: procurement@hedgewell.com
- Redwood owners: Ava Chen (AE), Ben Carter (Sales Eng), Monica Patel (Solutions Eng)

Proposed mutual onramp (high-level milestones):
- Milestone 1 — Pilot scope & success criteria agreed (Owners: Lena / Ava) — target date: 2027-04-05
- Milestone 2 — Data access & secure connectivity (VPC peering / SFTP + KMS test) (Owners: Mark / Monica) — target date: 2027-04-19
- Milestone 3 — Pilot run (1–2 weeks) + perf/cost baseline (Owners: Hedgewell / Redwood) — target date: 2027-05-03
- Milestone 4 — Exec review & procurement decision (Owners: Lena / procurement@hedgewell.com / Ava) — target date: 2027-05-17

Attachments: I am attaching a first draft MPA and an initial SLA sketch to make reviews easier: Hedgewell_MPA_v0.1.pdf, SLA-draft-Redwood.pdf.

Immediate asks / CTA:
- Can you confirm the owners and the proposed milestone dates?
- Please share the procurement contact (or confirm procurement@hedgewell.com) and any legal constraints on data residency we should be aware of.
- If the dates look tight, tell me which milestone to push out and we'll adjust.

I'll follow up with a one-page pricing sketch and a lightweight benchmark plan once you confirm the scope.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

[attachment: SLA-draft-Redwood.pdf (application/pdf)]

title: URGENT: ScaleCart production failures after gamma release — immediate rollback request
preview:
From: Jamie Park <jamie@scalecart.com>
To: ava.chen@redwood.ai
Cc: sre@scalecart.com
Date: 2027-02-20T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Production: spike in 5xx/429 immediately after gamma-v2-quant rollout

Ava —

We just pushed your gamma-v2-quant variant at 07:45 UTC for our product recommendations endpoint (canary -> full rollout per instructions). Within ~10–30 minutes we saw an error amplification: 5xx rate jumped from ~2.4% to 12.7% and 429s from ~0.1% to 9.3%. Latency tails spiked and clients started retrying aggressively, which made the issue worse.

We're cutting a handful of numbers from our monitoring:
- Peak 5xx window (07:58–08:05 UTC): 15%
- 429 count increased 90x relative to baseline in 20m
- P95 latency went from 180ms -> 1.1s

Attached are a trimmed packet of logs and a chart (support_logs_20270220.zip, error_rate_surge_chart.png). Our SRE (cc) is standing by. Please advise next steps — we need immediate rollback or a protected fallback. This is impacting checkout conversion for several merchants.

We need an executive-level contact; this is escalated in our ops channel.

Thanks,
Jamie Park
Head of Engineering, ScaleCart
---



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: jamie@scalecart.com, sre@scalecart.com
Date: 2027-02-20T08:20:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Production: spike in 5xx/429 immediately after gamma-v2-quant rollout — immediate assistance required

Team — forwarding ScaleCart's report. They rolled gamma-v2-quant at ~07:45 UTC and are seeing an error / retry amplification. They requested rollback if we can't stabilize quickly.

Quick asks:
- Hannah: can we enforce a routing fallback (pin their traffic to gamma-v1 variant) at the edge and add per-customer throttling?
- Rishi/Jonas: can you validate the model runtime/quantization kernel selection and look for regressions (memory/latency anomalies) tied to the v2 release?
- Marissa: please prep an exec note (1–2 bullets) I can send to VP of Sales if ScaleCart asks for exec escalation.

I've attached the logs they sent and a couple of our dashboards. I am starting a 30 minute war-room.

Attachments: support_logs_20270220.zip, error_rate_surge_chart.png

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Enterprise (Redwood Inference)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: jamie@scalecart.com
Date: 2027-02-20T08:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Production: spike in 5xx/429 — initial runtime triage (gamma-v2-quant)

Short summary of what I can see from the traces + our internal telemetry (INF-7241):
- We enabled an FP8-optimized kernel path for gamma-v2-quant that should have been gated for sequence lengths <= 256. ScaleCart traffic has bursts of long sequences (avg 420 tokens) which appears to trigger an untested code-path.
- Symptoms: per-worker KV-cache warm-up + kernel mismatch leads to increased memory pressure and occasional RPC failures (502s) and local queue backpressure -> 429s at the gateway.
- Evidence: 
  * kernel selection logs show fallback oscillation between fp8-opt and fallback-fp16 on the same requests (see runtime_kv_dump.txt attached).
  * OOM/eviction spikes aligned with the error window in region us-west-2.

Immediate mitigations I recommend (in order):
1) Roll gamma traffic back to gamma-v1 (float path) for ScaleCart only — this is fastest.
2) Disable the new fp8 kernel auto-selection flag (hotfix rollback) and redeploy runtime binary to canary before re-enabling.
3) As a protective measure, temporarily lower max_batch_size and disable cross-request batching for this tenant to reduce memory compaction pressure.

I'll open a dedicated infra/serving patch (INF-7241) and attach a minimal hotfix PR with the selection flag inverted. If someone can coordinate the rollback with edge routing (Hannah), I will prepare the binary rollback.

Rishi
Lead, Applied ML Systems — Redwood Inference


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: jamie@scalecart.com, sre@scalecart.com
Date: 2027-02-20T09:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Production: spike in 5xx/429 — actions taken / war room

War room update (actions taken 08:50–09:00 UTC):
- Edge routing: pinned ScaleCart traffic to gamma-v1 variant (traffic split set to 100% gamma-v1 for their tenant). This reduced new 5xxs within ~3 minutes.
- Rate limiting: applied a per-tenant ingress token bucket (burst=200 qps, sustained=80 qps) to blunt client retry amplification.
- Circuit breaker: enforced fast-fail after 4 consecutive RPC errors to avoid queue pileups.
- Canary paused: paused the gamma-v2 rollout globally until infra hotfix validated.

Next steps:
- Coordinate with Rishi to roll back fp8 kernel auto-select flag in a canary binary (he has the PR INF-7241).
- Schedule 30-min follow-up with ScaleCart ops at 09:30 UTC for validation.
- Collect a 10-minute window of detailed traces for the team to validate the kernel mismatch hypothesis.

I will keep the routing pin in place until we confirm stability. Paging Jonas for router-level logs if needed.

Hannah Schmitt
Principal SRE — Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jamie Park <jamie@scalecart.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-21T14:03:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Production: spike in 5xx/429 immediately after gamma-v2-quant rollout — summary and next steps

Jamie —

First, I am very sorry for the impact. Summary of what we did and next steps:

- Timeline: gamma-v2-quant rollout triggered errors on 2027-02-20 ~07:45 UTC. Team responded immediately and put ScaleCart on gamma-v1 by 08:50 UTC.
- Current status: ScaleCart traffic is pinned to gamma-v1. Error rates have returned to baseline; we are monitoring with you.
- Root cause (working hypothesis): a new FP8-optimized kernel selection path in gamma-v2-quant triggered on long sequence traffic and caused memory pressure / kernel oscillation -> 5xx / gateway 429s.

Planned follow-ups:
1) Rishi will ship a hotfix to disable the auto-select flag and run a canary for 2 hours (INF-7241).
2) We'll perform an extended soak test for sequences >400 tokens and a targeted perf run for ScaleCart's traffic profile.
3) We'll prepare an executive note and timeline for you and your leadership. Marissa will share that within 2 working hours.

We understand the business impact and will make a formal RCA once the hotfix is validated. If you'd like, we can schedule a 30-minute exec sync today at 17:00 PST; I will include our VP of Product.

Again — apologies and thank you for the quick coordination.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Enterprise, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0117

title: Bridge plan, go/no-go criteria, and cross-team commitments
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Nguyen <sarah@atlashealth.com>
Date: Sun, 14 Feb 2027 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Bridge plan, go/no-go criteria, and cross-team commitments

Team —

With QBR two weeks out, I ran a rapid assessment of our product/eng delivery readiness and attached a \"bridge plan\" with: a decision tree for go/no-go, owner-level commitments for the next two sprints, and rollback criteria for the launch path. High-level summary below and asks at the bottom.

Quick summary (TL;DR)
- Primary gating areas: feature parity for the Embed API, latency regression risk on >8k context, and infra capacity (Dedicated pools).
- Proposed go/no-go triggers: critical regressions in accuracy, >=10% latency SLO degradation for p95, or unresolved infra capacity shortfall >20% of forecast peak.
- Rollback criteria: if any of the above persist after an agreed 24h mitigation window, fail forward to the last stable model + feature flag.

Key open risks
- Engineering: KV cache regression when serving 8k seq workloads (unknown surface area in the quantized path).\
- Infra: Dedicated pool quotas show pinch points in eu-west during expected burst slices (see attached snapshot).
- Product: AtlasHealth requires a hard ETA for the privacy-edge integration for their prelaunch pilot.

A few concrete asks — please reply with owners and confirmations by EOD Monday (2027-02-15):
1) Confirm acceptance or propose edits to the go/no-go triggers above.
2) Identify a primary and backup owner for infra quota escalation (infra lead + backup).
3) Engineering to estimate mitigation ETA for KV cache regression (hours to fix + rollout plan).
4) Product (Laura) to confirm communication plan and any customer-facing mitigations for AtlasHealth.

Attached: bridge_plan_v2.xlsx (decision tree + owner matrix), infra_quota_snapshot.pdf

Thanks — I know this is tight, but we need clear ownership ahead of the QBR so leadership can sign the go/no-go.

— Ava Chen
Senior PM, Product Launches
Redwood Inference


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah@atlashealth.com>
Date: Sun, 14 Feb 2027 10:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Bridge plan, go/no-go criteria, and cross-team commitments

Thanks, Ava — solid framing. A few quick items from leadership perspective:

1) Go/no-go triggers: agree with the three bullets, please add one more explicit metric: any functional regression that causes a customer-visible failure for the top-3 customers (by ARR) = FAIL. For measurable SLOs add: p95 latency degradation >10% or absolute p95 >350ms (whichever is hit first).

2) Cost delta threshold: if mitigation or fallback increases per-token cost by >35% versus baseline, we need a formal exec sign-off before proceeding.

3) Infra quotas: we need an owner for quota escalations and a contact list for cloud provider escalation. Karthik — can you own the escalation path + the snapshot validation? Tessa, please add an opt-in for burst slices in the Dedicated pool policy so we can buy short-term headroom if needed.

4) Customer comms: Laura, please draft the AtlasHealth note explaining the contingency plan and expected dates. Keep it short and set expectations for the pilot.

I'll take point on exec decision if the triggers are met; but the more prescriptive and instrumented the checks are, the faster we can move.

— Vivek
CTO, Redwood Inference

On Sun, 14 Feb 2027 at 09:02, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> With QBR two weeks out, I ran a rapid assessment of our product/eng delivery readiness and attached a \"bridge plan\" with: a decision tree for go/no-go, owner-level commitments for the next two sprints, and rollback criteria for the launch path. High-level summary below and asks at the bottom.
> 
> [trimmed original for thread]


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah@atlashealth.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Feb 2027 11:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Bridge plan, go/no-go criteria, and cross-team commitments

Ava — ran a quick investigation against the quantized KV path and infra snapshot. Notes below and an ETA for a mitigation hack + follow-up plan.

Findings
- KV cache regression: root cause looks like an off-by-one eviction interaction when sequence length >6k in our quantized kernels. Repro is intermittent but consistent under synthetic load.
- Impact: 5–12% increase in p95 for the affected generator path in our tests. In production, it would likely spike higher under bursty workloads.
- Infra snapshot validation: Dedicated pool quotas show only ~65% headroom in eu-west at peak; we need to provision an additional 2x burst slices for the expected launch window.

Proposed immediate actions (owners + ETAs)
1) Short-term mitigation (engineering hotfix, deploy to canary): patch eviction index, mitigates ~80% of p95 regression. ETA: 36 hours. Owner: Karthik (primary), Dev Patel (backup).

Subject: Bridge plan, go/no-go criteria, and cross-team commitments

Team —

With QBR two weeks out, I ran a rapid assessment of our product/eng delivery readiness and attached a \"bridge plan\" with: a decision tree for go/no-go, owner-level commitments for the next two sprints, and rollback criteria for the launch path. High-level summary below and asks at the bottom.

Quick summary (TL;DR)
- Primary gating areas: feature parity for the Embed API, latency regression risk on >8k context, and infra capacity (Dedicated pools).
- Proposed go/no-go triggers: critical regressions in accuracy, >=10% latency SLO degradation for p95, or unresolved infra capacity shortfall >20% of forecast peak.
- Rollback criteria: if any of the above persist after an agreed 24h mitigation window, fail forward to the last stable model + feature flag.

Key open risks
- Engineering: KV cache regression when serving 8k seq workloads (unknown surface area in the quantized path).\
- Infra: Dedicated pool quotas show pinch points in eu-west during expected burst slices (see attached snapshot).
- Product: AtlasHealth requires a hard ETA for the privacy-edge integration for their prelaunch pilot.

A few concrete asks — please reply with owners and confirmations by EOD Monday (2027-02-15):
1) Confirm acceptance or propose edits to the go/no-go triggers above.
2) Identify a primary and backup owner for infra quota escalation (infra lead + backup).
3) Engineering to estimate mitigation ETA for KV cache regression (hours to fix + rollout plan).
4) Product (Laura) to confirm communication plan and any customer-facing mitigations for AtlasHealth.

Attached: bridge_plan_v2.xlsx (decision tree + owner matrix), infra_quota_snapshot.pdf

Thanks — I know this is tight, but we need clear ownership ahead of the QBR so leadership can sign the go/no-go.

— Ava Chen
Senior PM, Product Launches
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Bridge plan, go/no-go criteria, and cross-team commitments

Thanks, Ava — solid framing. A few quick items from leadership perspective:

1) Go/no-go triggers: agree with the three bullets, please add one more explicit metric: any functional regression that causes a customer-visible failure for the top-3 customers (by ARR) = FAIL. For measurable SLOs add: p95 latency degradation >10% or absolute p95 >350ms (whichever is hit first).

2) Cost delta threshold: if mitigation or fallback increases per-token cost by >35% versus baseline, we need a formal exec sign-off before proceeding.

3) Infra quotas: we need an owner for quota escalations and a contact list for cloud provider escalation. Karthik — can you own the escalation path + the snapshot validation? Tessa, please add an opt-in for burst slices in the Dedicated pool policy so we can buy short-term headroom if needed.

4) Customer comms: Laura, please draft the AtlasHealth note explaining the contingency plan and expected dates. Keep it short and set expectations for the pilot.

I'll take point on exec decision if the triggers are met; but the more prescriptive and instrumented the checks are, the faster we can move.

CTO, Redwood Inference

- Infra snapshot validation: Dedicated pool quotas show only ~65% headroom in eu-west at peak; we need to provision an additional 2x burst slices for the expected launch window.

title: Meridian Labs: security playbook for private deployment (VPC / air-gap asks)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>; secops@meridian-labs.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2027 15:12:00 +0000
Subject: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Hi Alice / Meridian security team,

Thanks again for taking the time today — great discussion. Below is a concise recap of what we heard + the follow-ups we promised. Please correct anything that doesn't match your internal notes.

Key items we captured
- PII scope: customer names, emails, order IDs, and clinician notes (free text) will be sent to Redwood in encrypted form for embeddings and generation.
- Retention ask: Meridian requires 30-day ephemeral retention for request/response payloads on the pilot, with logs retained for 180 days for audit.
- Logging: request-level audit logs are required (who called, model version, request_id). No full-text retention beyond 30 days unless explicitly approved.
- Residency/egress: pilot will run in a Redwood Private VPC in us-east-1. No external egress to third-party endpoints; outbound to Meridian endpoints allowed over approved peering tunnels only.
- Air-gap expectation: for a future on-prem iteration there may be a soft air-gap requirement — discussing feasibility and timelines separately.

Open technical clarification items (action owners):
1) PII redaction options: can Redwood redact stored embeddings automatically? (Action: Redwood — Ben to confirm embedding retention options)
2) KV/prefetch logging: how much token-level metadata is kept by the runtime? (Action: Redwood — Marcus to provide runtime logging matrix)
3) Network controls: can we enforce deny-by-default egress and granular port rules on the dedicated VPC? (Action: Redwood — Ava to produce VPC stub + recommended firewall rules)

Next steps and timeline
- Redwood to share an initial VPC network playbook and a one-page security checklist (today EOD). Attachment: redwood-arch-vpc-stub.pdf (stub).
- Meridian to share SOC extract and any mandatory CLA language (by end of week).
- Schedule a 45-min technical POC walk-through next week to validate egress and routing.

If that looks right I can convert these into a one-page mutual action plan.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Attachment: redwood-arch-vpc-stub.pdf (pdf)
Link: https://drive.redwood.com/s/meridian-vpc-playbook



From: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: secops@meridian-labs.com
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2027 09:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Ava — thanks for the detailed recap. This matches our notes. A few inline items and a doc drop: see answers below.

On Tue, 10 Feb 2027 at 15:12, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Key items we captured
> - PII scope: customer names, emails, order IDs, and clinician notes (free text) will be sent to Redwood in encrypted form for embeddings and generation.

Confirming: clinician notes are sensitive PHI; Meridian will only send them after DPA and willing to support encrypted-in-transit + at-rest keys. We will provide a limited dataset for the pilot (no more than 10k records).

> - Retention ask: Meridian requires 30-day ephemeral retention for request/response payloads on the pilot, with logs retained for 180 days for audit.

We expect retention control to be enforceable by tenant/config. Is this configurable per-deployment? If not, we need to know the manual process.

> - Logging: request-level audit logs are required (who called, model version, request_id). No full-text retention beyond 30 days unless explicitly approved.

We will send an extract from our internal SOC checklist; attaching a short excerpt that lists required audit fields. Attachment: meridian-soc-extract.pdf

> Open technical clarification items
> 1) PII redaction options: can Redwood redact stored embeddings automatically? (Action: Redwood — Ben to confirm embedding retention options)

Question for Redwood: do embeddings count as stored telemetry (i.e., subject to the 30-day retention) or do you treat embeddings as derived artifacts with separate retention?

> 2) KV/prefetch logging: how much token-level metadata is kept by the runtime? (Action: Redwood — Marcus to provide runtime logging matrix)

We need detail on whether KV cache keys or prefixes could contain identifiable tokens. If there is any chance, we need a plan to salt or hash keys.

> 3) Network controls: can we enforce deny-by-default egress and granular port rules on the dedicated VPC? (Action: Redwood — Ava to produce VPC stub + recommended firewall rules)

Yes — we require deny-by-default and explicit allow-lists. Also need to know whether the VPC supports private endpoints only (no NAT) and how we validate no unexpected outbound connections during the POC.

I think a short technical POC walkthrough focused on egress proofing and retention config will answer most items.

Best,
Alice Park
Director, IT Security — Meridian Labs
Attachment: meridian-soc-extract.pdf (pdf)



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2027 11:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Thanks Alice — good clarifications. A few technical clarifications from the runtime/retention side (short):

- Embeddings: treated as derived artifacts. By default we retain embeddings for 30 days for cache effectiveness, but we can enable a tenant-level policy to purge embeddings immediately after generation for the pilot. That purge can be automated via lifecycle policy.
- Logging matrix (summary): we record request_id, calling principal (API key or mTLS identity), model id/version, latency, token counts. We do NOT persist full request/response payloads beyond configured retention.
- KV cache: keys are deterministic by prefix + hashed request id. We can enable salted hashing to reduce collision/identifiability risk — Marcus will provide exact schema.
- Retention enforcement: configurable per-deployment; for Private VPC we run a policy agent that enforces lifecycle rules.

Actionable: Marcus, can you drop the runtime logging matrix (field names) to the shared drive so Alice can validate against your SOC fields?

Ava — our recommended minimal firewall policy for the pilot (high level):
- Deny all outbound by default
- Allow egress to Meridian CIDR blocks on TCP/443 only, and to Redwood internal monitoring endpoints over an approved peering (if agreed)
- Block DNS to public resolvers; provide a private resolver over the peering

I attached a short checklist spreadsheet we typically use to validate pilot readiness. Attachment: private-deploy-checklist.xlsx

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2027 09:24:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Alice / team — following up with the runtime artifacts + final pre-POC checklist.

On Thu, 12 Feb 2027 at 11:45, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> - KV cache: keys are deterministic by prefix + hashed request id. We can enable salted hashing to reduce collision/identifiability risk — Marcus will provide exact schema.

Attached: a short runtime logging matrix (fields listed), and a minimal test plan we'll run during the POC to validate egress controls and retention enforcement. Summary here:

Runtime logging matrix (high level)
- event_time
- tenant_id (hashed)
- request_id
- model_id
- caller_identity (mTLS CN or API key id)
- token_count_in
- token_count_out
- action (generate/embedding)

No full-text bodies are persisted beyond retention windows. We can hash tenant_id with a salt you control for extra protection.

Pre-POC checklist (we will run together):
1) Validate VPC peering and route tables (no NAT, deny-by-default egress enforced).
2) Verify TLS mTLS endpoints and confirm mutual authentication for admin APIs.
3) Run synthetic calls that include PII placeholders and confirm purge at 30s / 30 days (configurable).
4) Execute port-scan from inside VPC to ensure no unexpected outbound sockets.
5) Confirm monitoring endpoints are restricted to Redwood IPs (if Meridian requires zero external endpoints we will route monitoring over the peering).

Can we lock in a 45-min slot on Wed 17 Feb 2027 at 14:00 ET for the POC walk-through? If that works for Meridian I'll send a calendar invite and a short runbook.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Director, Runtime Engineering — Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com
Attachment: runtime-logging-matrix.csv (csv), private-deploy-checklist.xlsx (xlsx)
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/MD-2027-0421

title: ClearWave 8-week POC — success metrics, owners & next steps
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>, Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 Jan 2027 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Hi Sanjay and Claire,

Thanks again for a productive demo today. As promised, here’s a short recap and a proposed set of success criteria we can use to evaluate the 8-week POC. I focused on the areas you raised: latency, throughput, cost, and quality.

High-level asks / next steps:
- Confirm the sample dataset we should use (you mentioned ~25k anonymized queries).
- Sign the attached SOW so we can schedule reserved capacity for week 1.
- Confirm the stakeholder who will own evaluation and acceptance (QC/ML lead).

Proposed POC success KPIs (draft):
1) Latency: p95 generation time <= 220ms for 1–2k token prompts under our streamed response mode.
2) Throughput: baseline sustained 40 requests/sec with 80 concurrent KV-cache-backed sessions during busiest window.
3) Cost: average model cost <= $0.0007 per token (we’ll report token consumption and unit cost daily).
4) Quality: human-evaluated relevance >= 88% on your annotated 1k holdout set (sample rubric attached); automated BLEU/ROUGE comparisons for regression checks.
5) Reliability: <1% model error rate and endpoint availability >= 99.5% during the trial window.

Ownership (suggested):
- Redwood infra & perf: Marcus Lin (will own workload shaping, batching, and region routing)
- Evaluation & data: Rachel Kim (will coordinate test harness and human evaluation)
- Commercial & escalation: Ava Chen (single point of contact for delivery & contract items)

Tentative timeline (we can adjust after you confirm dataset and SOW):
- Week 0: SOW sign & data exchange (we provision dedicated pool)
- Week 1: Smoke tests + baseline profiling
- Week 2-3: Iteration on prompts & quantization profile; run throughput tests
- Week 4: Mid-POC checkpoint (metrics review; adjust acceptance thresholds if needed)
- Week 6-8: Final evaluation & acceptance decision

Attachments: ClearWave_POC_SOW.pdf, clearwave_sample_payloads.zip (sample queries + annotation guide)

Please confirm if these KPIs match what you discussed internally and who will be the acceptance owner on your side. I can push a signed SOW back today if the commercial terms look good.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198


From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Feb 2027 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks Ava — this is great and aligns with our expectations. A few clarifications from our side:
- Dataset: we’ll provide 30k anonymized user queries plus a 1k holdout labeled set by Tue (2/2).
- Acceptance owner: Claire will be primary for the quality pass; she wants to be looped on the daily automated reports.
- Latency bar: Claire asked if p95 can be targeted to 200ms instead of 220ms — how much headroom do you expect?

Action: I’ll review the SOW with procurement; if terms are unchanged we can sign by Tue EOD.

—Sanjay
Sanjay Patel
Head of Product
ClearWave Analytics


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Feb 2027 11:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Quick notes on feasibility:
- 200ms p95 is achievable for many of our LLM variants with streaming enabled, assuming avg prompt length stays under ~800 tokens and we route to an NVLink-backed pool. If they push longer prompts, latency jumps.
- Throughput: 40 req/sec sustained with 80 concurrent sessions is fine if we reserve a 4-GPU pool with continuous batching. Will coordinate with capacity team once SOW is signed.
- Cost: I can produce an expected cost/token table for two model classes (standard / low-cost quantized) by EOD Tue.

I’ll prep a short runbook for Claire that explains how we measure p95 and how to reproduce throughput tests.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Senior Infra Eng, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>, Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Feb 2027 09:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks Sanjay — that plan works. Claire, great to hear you’ll own the quality pass. A couple of operational notes:
- We measure p95 at the gateway after batching and just before byte encoding to reflect application latency. I’ll include the exact curl and k6 commands in the runbook.
- For quality: Rachel, can you confirm the rubric columns you need on the 1k holdout? I propose: Relevance (0–3), Factuality (0–1), and Response Appropriateness (0–2).

Next steps I’ll do once I get your go-ahead:
1) Send SOW for signature (DocuSign link)
2) Marcus will provision a 4-GPU reserved pool and post the capacity booking ETA
3) Rachel will get the evaluation harness spun up and share the annotation sheet by Wed EOD

Ava


From: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 03 Feb 2027 13:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks — that rubric looks good. Couple of tweaks from QC:
- Relevance threshold: we’ll require >= 88% where relevance is 2 or 3 on your 0–3 scale.
- Factuality: we’ll flag any hallucinations; acceptable threshold is <= 3% hallucination rate on the holdout.

We’ll send the annotated 1k by EOD Thu (2/4). We can live with the 220ms p95 if Redwood can show a clear path to 200ms by the final evaluation (we need to justify this to our product leadership).

Claire Johnson
Head of Data Science
ClearWave Analytics


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Feb 2027 09:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Perfect — thanks for the rubric and timeline. I’ve attached the DocuSign SOW (link below).
DocuSign: https://docusign.com/link/rw-clearwave-7421

I’ll have Marcus send the capacity confirmation once provisioned and Rachel will share the evaluation sheet today. Looking forward to kick-off next week.

Best,
Ava
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

title: Change order: credits repackage + SOW recast
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Procurement <procurement@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-18T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Change order: credits repackage + SOW recast

Hi Sanjay / procurement team,

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion, attached is a draft change order that: 
- Repackages part of the committed GPU capacity into a pool of burst credits (to smooth utilization spikes),
- Recasts Exhibit A (Implementation SOW) into two phases so Milestone 1 can be delivered earlier, and
- Applies a modest one-time credit to offset the implementation shift (see section 2.3).

What we need from you to move to signature:
1) Confirmation that the burst credits concept is acceptable (we've included a 90-day expiry on credits in the draft).
2) Clarify whether Procurement requires any alternate billing codes for the reclassified line items.
3) If you have redlines, please send them back — we can iterate quickly.

Attachments: Redwood_Change_Order_Draft_v3.docx, Exhibit-A-Recast.pdf

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0127
Redwood Inference — Production-first inference for product teams



From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@nimblehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Procurement <procurement@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-12-18T10:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Change order: credits repackage + SOW recast

Ava —

Thanks for the draft. We like the approach overall but have a few commercial/legal points to address before we can sign: 

- Credit expiry: 90 days feels short for our procurement cadence. Can we extend to 180 days?
- Pricing: Section 3 lists the per-hour committed rate blended into credits. Our finance team wants line-item pricing preserved for audit. Can you provide a reconciliation table that maps old committed units to new credits?
- SOW: The phase split is fine, but the acceptance criteria for Milestone 1 need to be tightened — we added language to the redline.

I've attached our redline (NH_Redlines_v2.docx). Laura/Legal will need to agree to the acceptance wording and the audit-friendly pricing layout.

If we can resolve those three items, I can get an internal approval path started.

Attachments: NH_Redlines_v2.docx

Regards,
Sanjay Rao
Head of Platform Ops | Nimble Health
sanjay.rao@nimblehealth.com



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-18T11:05:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Change order: credits repackage + SOW recast

Ava/Laura — forwarding Sanjay's redlines (attached). Quick notes: 

- Procurement is asking for 180-day expiry instead of 90. From a product/ops standpoint, 90 helps capacity re-optimization; from a commercial/close standpoint, 180 may be an acceptable concession if we limit total convertible credits.
- Finance wants a reconciliation table. I'll ask our billing ops to produce a simple mapping (committed GPU hours -> credit units -> equivalent $). Laura, can Legal review the Milestone acceptance wording and confirm whether the tightened language creates any operational risk?

Proposed next steps: 
1) Laura to review redline and flag any legal blockers (by EOD 12/18).
2) Ava to confirm whether we can do 180-day expiry with a cap on convertible credits (I can propose a cap of 25% of monthly committed baseline).
3) I'll get billing ops to produce the reconciliation table and attach to the adjusted change order.

Quote of Sanjay's message below for context:

--- On 2026-12-18 10:22, Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@nimblehealth.com> wrote: ---
> Ava —
>
> Thanks for the draft. We like the approach overall but have a few commercial/legal points to address...
> Attachments: NH_Redlines_v2.docx

Karthik
VP, Sales Strategy | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com



From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-20T16:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Change order: credits repackage + SOW recast

Sanjay —

Appreciate the quick review and helpful redlines. We've made the following updates and attached the updated change order: 

- Credit expiry extended to 180 days (Section 2.1) but with a cap: no more than 25% of the customer's monthly committed baseline can be converted into burst credits in any rolling 30-day window. This protects our capacity planning while addressing your finance team's concern.
- Reconciliation table inserted as Appendix B (maps previous committed GPU-hours to credit units and shows the blended $ equivalence).
- Acceptance criteria for Milestone 1 tightened per your language; Laura has reviewed the wording and added an operational fallback clause (Section 4.2) that preserves acceptance testing but allows a 7-day remediation window.

Attachments: Redwood_Change_Order_Draft_v3.docx (updated), Exhibit-A-Recast.pdf, Appendix-B-Reconciliation.xlsx (attached to Drive link)

If this looks good, I can route the final doc to DocuSign today and mark the expiration window in the SOW. If Procurement wants a short call to sign off on the reconciliation table, I can set one up for tomorrow morning PST.

Next steps for us: 
- Sanjay: please confirm acceptance of the 25% cap and the 180-day expiry.
- Laura: confirm you are comfortable with the fallback/7-day remediation language for ops.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0127

On Fri, Dec 18, 2026 at 11:05 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Ava/Laura — forwarding Sanjay's redlines (attached). Quick notes...
>
> Karthik
> VP, Sales Strategy | Redwood Inference

title: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Emma Russo <emma.russo@merchantco.eu>, AP Finance <ap@clientco.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 09:07:00 +0000
Subject: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

Hi Emma / AP team,

Thanks again for the productive call last week. We want to make sure Redwood invoices and payment routing are aligned with MerchantCo EU's VAT residency to avoid delays on settlement. A few items we need to confirm before we finalize the December invoice: 

- Can you confirm the VAT ID that should appear on the invoice and provide the official VAT certificate (PDF)?
- Is MerchantCo set up to receive self-billed invoices, or do you require Redwood to issue invoices that include VAT and a reverse-charge notation?
- If reverse-charge applies, would your AP team accept a single invoice with the reverse-charge treatment, or do you prefer we split platform fees vs pass-through charges into separate lines/invoices for cross-border reporting?

If you can share the VAT certificate and the AP contact for routing, we'll generate a provisional settlement matrix and sample invoice for your review.

Appreciate a quick confirmation — we have a payment window next week and want to prevent a hold.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: none


From: Emma Russo <emma.russo@merchantco.eu>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Finance <ap@clientco.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 11:24:00 +0000
Subject: Re: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the note — attaching our VAT certificate. MerchantCo EU is registered in Germany: VAT DE274839102. Please use that ID on invoices. We are NOT set up for self-billing from Redwood at the moment; our AP requires vendor-issued invoices with a clear tax treatment line.

On reverse-charge: we typically accept the reverse-charge annotation on inbound invoices for B2B cross-border services, but our AP has asked for the following to avoid manual holds:

- Invoice must show: our VAT ID, vendor VAT ID, a statement: \"Reverse-charge - EU VAT to be accounted for by the customer per Article 194 EU VAT Directive\"
- A separate invoice line for reimbursable pass-through costs (if any) with net amounts only
- PDF copy of VAT certificate attached to the payment request (helps automated checks)

AP contact for routing and validation: finance.ap@merchantco.eu (they run the automated VAT checks).

I've attached MerchantCo_VAT_certificate.pdf.

If it helps, happy to join a 20-mins call tomorrow to walk through the fields.

Best,
Emma Russo
Head of Finance Operations
MerchantCo EU
emma.russo@merchantco.eu

title: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Hi Liam —

Thanks for taking the call earlier. Attached is an initial slab model (initial-slab-model.xlsx) that maps rough tokens/sec requirements to GPU slabs under a couple of modeling assumptions. High level: we built three scenarios (Conservative / Expected / Aggressive) using your ballpark numbers from the demo.

Quick asks so we can lock numbers:
- 95th% concurrent inference sessions during peak 10-minute windows (rough count)
- Avg tokens per request (our model needs both prompt + response avg)
- Peak hours (timezone/day) and whether peaks are predictable or ad-hoc
- Target latency SLO (p95) you want to guarantee for the commitment

Key modeling assumptions in the sheet (to review):
- Serving model: picking a production-optimized variant (redwood-3.1b-qat) as baseline
- Quantization: int8 path for committed slabs
- Batching: conservative batching window = 10ms; optimistic = 30ms
- KV cache hit assumed = 45% for chat workloads (affects effective tokens/sec)

Ballpark numbers from the conservative column: ~38k tokens/sec sustained across the committed slab (with a 1.4x headroom factor for bursts). The spreadsheet contains per-GPU effective tokens/sec based on our microbenchmarks.

I included Ben on this thread — he'll call out a recommendation for headroom and failover allocation. If you can share the short profile above by end of day Thursday we can iterate and push a formal slab recommendation + an ops runbook for cold-starts and cache warmups.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood-inference.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachment: initial-slab-model.xlsx (Excel, 120KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Dec 2026 12:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Adding a few modeling clarifications / assumptions from infra side:

- Model profile: if SpectrumLytics plans to use a 7B class (quantized) vs a 70B class there is ~4-6x difference in effective tokens/s per GPU. Please confirm target family.
- Kernel optimizations: we modeled int8 quant paths; fp16 paths require ~20-30% more GPU time per token.
- Batching/window: effective tokens/sec jumps non-linearly with a 20–40ms batching allowance; if you cannot accept additional latency for batching we drop into lower throughput per GPU.
- KV cache: recommend instrumenting a short warmup period in canary to validate predicted 40–60% cache hit rate.

Recommendation for committed headroom: plan for a 1.5x safety factor on top of expected sustained tokens/sec for Q1 (this buys capacity for seasonality and burst patterns). For burst absorption (unplanned peaks) we recommend a 2-node hot standby pool in a different AZ to preserve failover SLA.

I attached a short note with compute-footprint assumptions. If you want, we can run a quick synthetic that maps your sample trace to GPU-days and tokens/month.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Capacity Planning | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood-inference.com

Attachment: compute-footprint-notes.pdf (PDF, 45KB)

From: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Wed, 8 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Hi Ava / Ben —

Thanks for the model. Apologies for the slow reply — procurement + security had follow-ups. Here are the data points you asked for (short answers):
- 95th% concurrent sessions (10-min window): ~320 sessions
- Avg tokens per request (prompt + response): 420 tokens
- Peak windows: Mon–Fri 09:00–11:00 PT and 17:00–19:00 PT; predictable but with occasional spikes around campaign launches
- Target p95 latency SLO: 350ms (we can accept up to 400ms during known campaigns)

Additional notes: our legal team prefers a two-step procurement: (1) pilot month with reduced commit, (2) 12-month dedicated commitment after pilot. Procurement referenced HubSpot deal SL-2026-042 and sent a DocuSign to contracts@spectrumlytics.com.

If you can translate the numbers into a minimal committed slab for the pilot (supporting predictable peaks with transparent burst buffer), that would help us accelerate internal approvals.

On the model family: we plan to run a mix of medium 13B quantized variants for conversational agents and a small set of 70B for complex summarization calls (~10% of traffic).

Thanks,
Liam O'Neil
VP Product | SpectrumLytics
liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com

-----Original Message-----
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>
Sent: Tue, Dec 7, 2026 at 09:12 AM
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Subject: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

(quoted for context)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Dec 2026 15:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Liam —

Thanks for the details; that profile is very helpful. Below is a concise recommendation tailored for the pilot -> annual commit path you described. I’ve attached a final slab recommendation spreadsheet that converts your token profile into per-GPU counts and an ops checklist for cold-starts / cache warmups.

Executive recommendation (pilot -> commit):
- Pilot (30 days): 3x H100-equivalent GPUs in the dedicated pool + 1x A100 for canary; configured with int8 quant path for 13B family and fp16 fallback for 70B calls. Expected sustainable throughput (pilot config): ~12–14k tokens/sec across the pool with ability to burst to ~22k tokens/sec for short windows using short-lived burst nodes.
- Annual committed slab (recommended): 10x H100-equivalent GPUs (primary slab) + 2x A100 for cross-AZ failover. With a 1.5x headroom factor this maps to an expected sustained capacity of ~40–42k tokens/sec (covers regular peaks & seasonal growth).

Why this split: H100-class nodes give better peak token/s for your 13B quantized workload and improve latency for small-response chat flows. A100 reserve nodes are cheaper and act as durable failover for longer-running summarization jobs (70B flows) that we fall back to fp16 on.

Operational notes:
- Cold-starts & cache warmup: warm the KV cache with synthetic traffic in the first 48hrs of pilot; the ops checklist in the spreadsheet has exact sample curl sequences.
- Latency SLO mapping: with the pilot pool we expect p95 ~330–360ms for standard chat; with the annual slab p95 should be ~280–340ms depending on burst windows. We will instrument tracing and token-level cost breakdowns in the console.
- Billing/commit: we can phase the commit to start billing on day 31 for the annual slab if pilot metrics meet agreed thresholds.

Next steps I propose:
1) We lock the pilot slab and run a 48hr synthetic traffic run to validate our KV hit assumptions (can do this as early as next week).
2) If synth numbers match (within 10%), we provision the pilot and share a runbook and rollback plan.
3) Marissa, can you coordinate procurement artifacts and the DocuSign route?

Marissa will pull in contracts and finalize the DocuSign envelope. If you’re comfortable with this direction I’ll update the HubSpot deal with the proposed slab counts and attach the spreadsheet.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood-inference.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Marissa Cole
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com

Attachment: final-slab-recommendation.xlsx (Excel, 95KB)

title: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@stoneanalytics.com>, Nina Gómez <nina.gomez@stoneanalytics.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)

Hi Samir / Nina — thanks again for the discovery call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before kickoff: 

Summary of needs (from today)
- Real-time summarization for analyst notes (streaming output, sub-250ms per chunk)
- Embeddings index for semantic search across 18 months of reports (~200k documents)
- Tool calling to surface BI queries against their SQL layer and callouts to the document store

Success criteria we discussed
1) Latency: 95th perc token latency <= 300ms in our hosted trial environment
2) Throughput: support bursts up to 80 concurrent streaming sessions
3) Quality: key metric is F1 >= 0.78 on intent-extraction test set (we'll exchange test prompts)
4) Integration: SDK-based ingest + API streaming for summarization working end-to-end within the 4-week trial

Proposed trial plan (high level)
- Week 0 (this week): Share trial runbook + sample prompts, agree on test-set and credentials
- Week 1: SDK ingest + embeddings index build; validate sample queries
- Week 2: Streaming summarization + tool-calling connector to their SQL test DB; run initial evals
- Week 3: Iterate on prompt/templates, quantization recommendations; measure cost per 1k tokens
- Week 4: Final evaluation, handoff to CS for production planning or Dedicated sizing if it meets criteria

Owners
- Redwood technical lead (integration): Dylan Brooks
- Redwood runtime tuning & cost: Marcus Lin
- Customer PM: Samir Patel / Nina Gómez

Action items (immediate)
- Redwood: share the trial runbook + sample-api-spec (see attachments) — I attached the initial runbook and an API snippet.
- Stone: share test credentials (read-only SQL user + S3 sample access) and the evaluation prompt set by EOD Monday
- Schedule kickoff tech-sync: propose Tue 2026-12-08 09:00 PT (please confirm)

Attachments: stone-trial-tech-runbook.xlsx (workbook), sample-api-spec.md (snippet)

Thanks — happy to adjust timeline based on your ops window.

Best,
Ava Chen
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0124

---
Note: I included the streaming recommendation (chunk size 512 tokens, continuous KV caching) under the runbook tab.

- Postgres connector: I'll drop a sample in the shared Drive and include a minimal Docker-based harness that returns JSON rows for the agent to consume.

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>

Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>

Subject: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)

Hi Samir / Nina — thanks again for the discovery call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before kickoff: 

Summary of needs (from today)
- Real-time summarization for analyst notes (streaming output, sub-250ms per chunk)
- Embeddings index for semantic search across 18 months of reports (~200k documents)
- Tool calling to surface BI queries against their SQL layer and callouts to the document store

Success criteria we discussed
1) Latency: 95th perc token latency <= 300ms in our hosted trial environment
2) Throughput: support bursts up to 80 concurrent streaming sessions
3) Quality: key metric is F1 >= 0.78 on intent-extraction test set (we'll exchange test prompts)
4) Integration: SDK-based ingest + API streaming for summarization working end-to-end within the 4-week trial

Proposed trial plan (high level)
- Week 0 (this week): Share trial runbook + sample prompts, agree on test-set and credentials
- Week 1: SDK ingest + embeddings index build; validate sample queries
- Week 2: Streaming summarization + tool-calling connector to their SQL test DB; run initial evals
- Week 3: Iterate on prompt/templates, quantization recommendations; measure cost per 1k tokens
- Week 4: Final evaluation, handoff to CS for production planning or Dedicated sizing if it meets criteria

Owners
- Redwood technical lead (integration): Dylan Brooks
- Redwood runtime tuning & cost: Marcus Lin
- Customer PM: Samir Patel / Nina Gómez

Action items (immediate)
- Redwood: share the trial runbook + sample-api-spec (see attachments) — I attached the initial runbook and an API snippet.
- Stone: share test credentials (read-only SQL user + S3 sample access) and the evaluation prompt set by EOD Monday
- Schedule kickoff tech-sync: propose Tue 2026-12-08 09:00 PT (please confirm)

Attachments: stone-trial-tech-runbook.xlsx (workbook), sample-api-spec.md (snippet)

Thanks — happy to adjust timeline based on your ops window.

Best,
Ava Chen
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0124

title: ShopFront checkout: urgent throughput & p99 spike
preview:
From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@shopfront.com
Date: 2026-12-05T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Hi Ava,

We're seeing a significant degradation in checkout performance this morning during our peak window (08:45–09:15 PST). Key symptoms: 
- p95 steady but p99 jumped from ~450ms to ~2.8s starting ~08:50.
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on checkout API for ~15 minutes.
- throughput (requests/sec) held roughly equal — not a traffic surge.

This is impacting conversions on the storefront. I've attached traces and a small set of sampled p99 payloads. Can you triage and advise whether this is on your side (model serving) or ours (request patterns)?

Attachments: [shopfront-checkout-traces.zip (1.2MB)], [p99-samples.txt]

Thanks — need a quick heads-up to share with our ops team.

— Liam
Head of Platform, ShopFront
liam.oneill@shopfront.com


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:26:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Marcus / Rishi — forwarding an urgent report from ShopFront (Deal SF-4721). They see p99 spikes and 5xxs during their checkout peak; traffic didn't jump. Attached traces from customer. Can you: 
1) Triage root cause (queueing/backpressure/concurrency)
2) Apply short-term mitigations we can push in the next 15–30m

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Liam — thanks for the data and flagging this. We're on it. Quick update for your ops: 
- We've started a limited mitigation: enabling an immediate fallback to the smaller model variant (redwood-lite-1b) for checkout route to reduce per-request CPU.
- We've also temporarily set a conservative concurrency cap for your account route to 50 concurrent requests to ease queueing.
- SRE is investigating whether backpressure from long-context KV cache churn caused worker stalls.

Expect a follow-up in ~30m with concrete metrics. If the mitigations reduce errors, we'll propose a short credit for the affected window while we complete the postmortem.

Action items I own: confirm fallbacks applied, monitor p99/p50/p95 and 5xx, propose credit amount.

— Marcus
Senior Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:56:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Marcus/Ava — quick technical summary and what we changed (executed at 09:40 PST):
Findings (initial):
- Root cause appears to be KV cache churn on long sequences for a subset of checkout requests; those requests incur heavy KV cache recompute and push worker threads into long-tail processing.
- Concurrency cap for ShopFront route was set higher than intended in a config merge last week (cap=250 vs intended 75). During a steady request rate this allowed many requests to queue behind long-running ops, inflating p99.
- Backpressure from workers wasn't being propagated cleanly to the edge router due to an internal circuit tolerance mismatch.

Immediate mitigations applied:
- Reduced concurrency cap to 50 (config pushed at 09:40).
- Enabled aggressive prefix/KV caching for the checkout route to reduce recompute for repeated context.
- Activated fallback routing to redwood-lite-1b for very long contexts (threshold 2k tokens).
- Throttled large-context non-checkout requests for 30m.

Result so far: p99 has dropped from the 2.8s peak down to ~620ms within 12 minutes; 5xx rate down to 0.9%. Monitoring continues.

Next steps (post-incident):
- Root-cause postmortem and fix for the config drift that set the cap to 250.
- Improve backpressure propagation in edge router (SRE epic).
- Add an automated alert when route cap diverges from policy.

Links: metrics dashboard: https://monitoring.redwood.ai/d/shopsf/SF-4721-latency

I'll draft the technical notes for the postmortem — Marcus/Ava please let me know wording for customer update.

— Rishi
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>

2) Credits: I pre-approved a goodwill credit up to 10% for the billing period pending Marcus's recommendation.
3) Postmortem owners: Rishi please lead the RCA; include a timeline and the config-drift remediation plan.
4) Comms: I'll notify Sales leadership and CS once the final text is ready.

Let's finalize the customer note by 09:00 PST tomorrow so ShopFront can communicate internally.

— Evelyn
VP, Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
